sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच धर्मक्षेत्रे कुरुक्षेत्रे समवेता युयुत्सवः। मामकाः पाण्डवाश्चैव किमकुर्वत सञ्जय ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Dhritarâshtra said: On the holy plain, on the field of Kuru, gathered together, eager for battle, what did they, O Sanjaya, my people and the Pândavas?
transliteration: dhṛitarāśhtra uvācha dharma-kṣhetre kuru-kṣhetre samavetā yuyutsavaḥ māmakāḥ pāṇḍavāśhchaiva kimakurvata sañjaya

sanskrit: सञ्जय उवाच दृष्ट्वा तु पाण्डवानीकं व्यूढं दुर्योधनस्तदा। आचार्यमुपसङ्गम्य राजा वचनमब्रवीत् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Sanjaya said: Having seen arrayed the army of the Pândavas, the Prince Duryodhana approached his teacher, and spake these words:
transliteration: sañjaya uvācha dṛiṣhṭvā tu pāṇḍavānīkaṁ vyūḍhaṁ duryodhanastadā āchāryamupasaṅgamya rājā vachanamabravīt

sanskrit: पश्यैतां पाण्डुपुत्राणामाचार्य महतीं चमूम्। व्यूढां द्रुपदपुत्रेण तव शिष्येण धीमता ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: "Behold this mighty host of the sons of Pându, O teacher, arrayed by the son of Drupada, thy wise disciple.
transliteration: paśhyaitāṁ pāṇḍu-putrāṇām āchārya mahatīṁ chamūm vyūḍhāṁ drupada-putreṇa tava śhiṣhyeṇa dhīmatā

sanskrit: अत्र शूरा महेष्वासा भीमार्जुनसमा युधि। युयुधानो विराटश्च द्रुपदश्च महारथः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Heroes are these, mighty bowmen, to Bhîma and Arjuna equal in battle: Yuyudhâna, Virâta, and Drupada of the great car.
transliteration: atra śhūrā maheṣhvāsā bhīmārjuna-samā yudhi yuyudhāno virāṭaśhcha drupadaśhcha mahā-rathaḥ

sanskrit: धृष्टकेतुश्चेकितानः काशिराजश्च वीर्यवान्। पुरुजित्कुन्तिभोजश्च शैब्यश्च नरपुङ्गवः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Drishtaketu, Chekitâna and the valiant King of Kâshi, Purujit and Kuntibhoja, and Shaivya, bull among men;
transliteration: dhṛiṣhṭaketuśhchekitānaḥ kāśhirājaśhcha vīryavān purujit kuntibhojaśhcha śhaibyaśhcha nara-puṅgavaḥ yudhāmanyuśhcha vikrānta uttamaujāśhcha vīryavān

sanskrit: युधामन्युश्च विक्रान्त उत्तमौजाश्च वीर्यवान्। सौभद्रो द्रौपदेयाश्च सर्व एव महारथाः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Yudhâmanyu the strong, and Uttamaujas the brave; Saubhadra and the Draupadeyas, all of great cars.
transliteration: saubhadro draupadeyāśhcha sarva eva mahā-rathāḥ

sanskrit: अस्माकं तु विशिष्टा ये तान्निबोध द्विजोत्तम। नायका मम सैन्यस्य संज्ञार्थं तान्ब्रवीमि ते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Know further all those who are our chiefs, O best of the twice-born, the leaders of my army; these I name to thee for thy information:
transliteration: asmākaṁ tu viśhiṣhṭā ye tānnibodha dwijottama nāyakā mama sainyasya sanjñārthaṁ tānbravīmi te

sanskrit: भवान्भीष्मश्च कर्णश्च कृपश्च समितिञ्जयः। अश्वत्थामा विकर्णश्च सौमदत्तिस्तथैव च ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Thou, Lord, and Bhîshma, and Karna and Kripa, conquering in battle; Ashvatthâmâ, Vikarna, and Saumadatti also;
transliteration: bhavānbhīṣhmaśhcha karṇaśhcha kṛipaśhcha samitiñjayaḥ aśhvatthāmā vikarṇaśhcha saumadattis tathaiva cha

sanskrit: अन्ये च बहवः शूरा मदर्थे त्यक्तजीविताः। नानाशस्त्रप्रहरणाः सर्वे युद्धविशारदाः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: And many others, heroes, for my sake renouncing their lives, with divers weapons and missiles, and all well-skilled in war.
transliteration: anye cha bahavaḥ śhūrā madarthe tyaktajīvitāḥ nānā-śhastra-praharaṇāḥ sarve yuddha-viśhāradāḥ

sanskrit: अपर्याप्तं तदस्माकं बलं भीष्माभिरक्षितम्। पर्याप्तं त्विदमेतेषां बलं भीमाभिरक्षितम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Yet insufficient seems this army of ours, though marshalled by Bhîshma, while that army of theirs seems sufficient, though marshalled by Bhîma;
transliteration: aparyāptaṁ tadasmākaṁ balaṁ bhīṣhmābhirakṣhitam paryāptaṁ tvidameteṣhāṁ balaṁ bhīmābhirakṣhitam

sanskrit: अयनेषु च सर्वेषु यथाभागमवस्थिताः। भीष्ममेवाभिरक्षन्तु भवन्तः सर्व एव हि ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Therefore in the rank and file let all, standing firmly in their respective divisions, guard Bhîshma, even all ye generals.
transliteration: ayaneṣhu cha sarveṣhu yathā-bhāgamavasthitāḥ bhīṣhmamevābhirakṣhantu bhavantaḥ sarva eva hi

sanskrit: तस्य संजनयन्हर्षं कुरुवृद्धः पितामहः। सिंहनादं विनद्योच्चैः शङ्खं दध्मौ प्रतापवान् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: To enhearten him, the Ancient of the Kurus, the Grandsire, the glorious, blew his conch, sounding on high a lion's roar.
transliteration: tasya sañjanayan harṣhaṁ kuru-vṛiddhaḥ pitāmahaḥ siṁha-nādaṁ vinadyochchaiḥ śhaṅkhaṁ dadhmau pratāpavān

sanskrit: ततः शङ्खाश्च भेर्यश्च पणवानकगोमुखाः। सहसैवाभ्यहन्यन्त स शब्दस्तुमुलोऽभवत् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Then conches and kettledrums, tabors and drums and cowhorns, suddenly blared forth, and the sound was tumultuous.
transliteration: tataḥ śhaṅkhāśhcha bheryaśhcha paṇavānaka-gomukhāḥ sahasaivābhyahanyanta sa śhabdastumulo ’bhavat

sanskrit: ततः श्वेतैर्हयैर्युक्ते महति स्यन्दने स्थितौ। माधवः पाण्डवश्चैव दिव्यौ शङ्खौ प्रदध्मतुः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Then, stationed in their great war-chariot, yoked to white horses, Mâdhava and the son of Pându blew their divine conches.
transliteration: tataḥ śhvetairhayairyukte mahati syandane sthitau mādhavaḥ pāṇḍavaśhchaiva divyau śhaṅkhau pradadhmatuḥ

sanskrit: पाञ्चजन्यं हृषीकेशो देवदत्तं धनंजयः। पौण्ड्रं दध्मौ महाशङ्खं भीमकर्मा वृकोदरः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Pânchajanya by Hrishîkesha, and Devadatta by Dhananjaya. Vrikodara of terrible deeds blew his mighty conch, Paundra;
transliteration: pāñchajanyaṁ hṛiṣhīkeśho devadattaṁ dhanañjayaḥ pauṇḍraṁ dadhmau mahā-śhaṅkhaṁ bhīma-karmā vṛikodaraḥ

sanskrit: अनन्तविजयं राजा कुन्तीपुत्रो युधिष्ठिरः। नकुलः सहदेवश्च सुघोषमणिपुष्पकौ ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: The King Yudhishthira, the son of Kuntî, blew Anantavijaya; Nakula and Sahadeva, Sughosha and Manipushpaka.
transliteration: anantavijayaṁ rājā kuntī-putro yudhiṣhṭhiraḥ nakulaḥ sahadevaśhcha sughoṣha-maṇipuṣhpakau

sanskrit: काश्यश्च परमेष्वासः शिखण्डी च महारथः। धृष्टद्युम्नो विराटश्च सात्यकिश्चापराजितः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: And Kâshya, of the great bow and Shikhandî, the mighty car-warrior, Drishtadyumna and Virâta and Sâtyaki, the unconquered.
transliteration: kāśhyaśhcha parameṣhvāsaḥ śhikhaṇḍī cha mahā-rathaḥ dhṛiṣhṭadyumno virāṭaśhcha sātyakiśh chāparājitaḥ

sanskrit: द्रुपदो द्रौपदेयाश्च सर्वशः पृथिवीपते। सौभद्रश्च महाबाहुः शङ्खान्दध्मुः पृथक्पृथक् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Drupada and the Draupadeyas, O Lord of earth, and Saubhadra, the mighty-armed, on all sides their several conches blew.
transliteration: drupado draupadeyāśhcha sarvaśhaḥ pṛithivī-pate saubhadraśhcha mahā-bāhuḥ śhaṅkhāndadhmuḥ pṛithak pṛithak

sanskrit: स घोषो धार्तराष्ट्राणां हृदयानि व्यदारयत्। नभश्च पृथिवीं चैव तुमुलो व्यनुनादयन् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: That tumultuous uproar rent the hearts of the sons of Dhritarâshtra, filling the earth and sky with sound.
transliteration: sa ghoṣho dhārtarāṣhṭrāṇāṁ hṛidayāni vyadārayat nabhaśhcha pṛithivīṁ chaiva tumulo nunādayan

sanskrit: अथ व्यवस्थितान् दृष्ट्वा धार्तराष्ट्रान्कपिध्वजः। प्रवृत्ते शस्त्रसंपाते धनुरुद्यम्य पाण्डवः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Then, beholding the sons of Dhritarâshtra standing arrayed, and flight of missiles about to begin, he whose crest is an ape, the son of Pându, took up his bow,
transliteration: atha vyavasthitān dṛiṣhṭvā dhārtarāṣhṭrān kapi-dhwajaḥ pravṛitte śhastra-sampāte dhanurudyamya pāṇḍavaḥ hṛiṣhīkeśhaṁ tadā vākyam idam āha mahī-pate

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच हृषीकेशं तदा वाक्यमिदमाह महीपते। सेनयोरुभयोर्मध्ये रथं स्थापय मेऽच्युत ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: And spake this word to Hrishîkesha, O Lord of Earth: Arjuna said: In the midst, between the two armies, stay my chariot, O Achyuta,
transliteration: arjuna uvācha senayor ubhayor madhye rathaṁ sthāpaya me ’chyuta

sanskrit: यावदेतान्निरीक्षेऽहं योद्धुकामानवस्थितान्। कैर्मया सह योद्धव्यमस्मिन्रणसमुद्यमे ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: That I may behold these standing, longing for battle, with whom I must strive in this outbreaking war,
transliteration: yāvadetān nirīkṣhe ’haṁ yoddhu-kāmān avasthitān kairmayā saha yoddhavyam asmin raṇa-samudyame

sanskrit: योत्स्यमानानवेक्षेऽहं य एतेऽत्र समागताः। धार्तराष्ट्रस्य दुर्बुद्धेर्युद्धे प्रियचिकीर्षवः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: And gaze on those here gathered together ready to fight, desirous of pleasing in battle the evil-minded son of Dhritarâshtra.
transliteration: yotsyamānān avekṣhe ’haṁ ya ete ’tra samāgatāḥ dhārtarāṣhṭrasya durbuddher yuddhe priya-chikīrṣhavaḥ

sanskrit: संजय उवाच एवमुक्तो हृषीकेशो गुडाकेशेन भारत। सेनयोरुभयोर्मध्ये स्थापयित्वा रथोत्तमम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Sanjaya said: Thus addressed by Gudâkesha, Hrishîkesha, O Bhârata, having stayed that best of chariots in the midst, between the two armies,
transliteration: sañjaya uvācha evam ukto hṛiṣhīkeśho guḍākeśhena bhārata senayor ubhayor madhye sthāpayitvā rathottamam

sanskrit: भीष्मद्रोणप्रमुखतः सर्वेषां च महीक्षिताम्। उवाच पार्थ पश्यैतान्समवेतान्कुरूनिति ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Over against Bhîshma, Drona and all the rulers of the world, said: "O Pârtha, behold these Kurus gathered together."
transliteration: bhīṣhma-droṇa-pramukhataḥ sarveṣhāṁ cha mahī-kṣhitām uvācha pārtha paśhyaitān samavetān kurūn iti

sanskrit: तत्रापश्यत्स्थितान्पार्थः पितृ़नथ पितामहान्। आचार्यान्मातुलान्भ्रातृ़न्पुत्रान्पौत्रान्सखींस्तथा ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Then saw Pârtha standing there, uncles and grandfathers, teachers, mother's brothers, cousins, sons and grandsons, comrades,
transliteration: tatrāpaśhyat sthitān pārthaḥ pitṝīn atha pitāmahān āchāryān mātulān bhrātṝīn putrān pautrān sakhīṁs tathā śhvaśhurān suhṛidaśh chaiva senayor ubhayor api

sanskrit: श्वशुरान्सुहृदश्चैव सेनयोरुभयोरपि। तान्समीक्ष्य स कौन्तेयः सर्वान्बन्धूनवस्थितान् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Fathers-in-law and benefactors also in both armies; seeing all these kinsmen thus standing arrayed, Kaunteya,
transliteration: tān samīkṣhya sa kaunteyaḥ sarvān bandhūn avasthitān kṛipayā parayāviṣhṭo viṣhīdann idam abravīt

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच कृपया परयाऽऽविष्टो विषीदन्निदमब्रवीत्। दृष्ट्वेमं स्वजनं कृष्ण युयुत्सुं समुपस्थितम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Deeply moved to pity, thus uttered in sadness: Arjuna said: Seeing these my kinsmen, O Krishna, arrayed, eager to fight,
transliteration: arjuna uvācha dṛiṣhṭvemaṁ sva-janaṁ kṛiṣhṇa yuyutsuṁ samupasthitam

sanskrit: सीदन्ति मम गात्राणि मुखं च परिशुष्यति। वेपथुश्च शरीरे मे रोमहर्षश्च जायते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: My limbs fail and my mouth is parched, my body quivers, and my hair stands on end,
transliteration: sīdanti mama gātrāṇi mukhaṁ cha pariśhuṣhyati vepathuśh cha śharīre me roma-harṣhaśh cha jāyate

sanskrit: गाण्डीवं स्रंसते हस्तात्त्वक्चैव परिदह्यते। न च शक्नोम्यवस्थातुं भ्रमतीव च मे मनः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Gândîva slips from my hand, and my skin burns all over, I am not able to stand, my mind is whirling,
transliteration: gāṇḍīvaṁ sraṁsate hastāt tvak chaiva paridahyate na cha śhaknomy avasthātuṁ bhramatīva cha me manaḥ

sanskrit: निमित्तानि च पश्यामि विपरीतानि केशव। न च श्रेयोऽनुपश्यामि हत्वा स्वजनमाहवे ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: And I see adverse omens, O Keshava. Nor do I foresee any advantage from slaying kinsmen in battle.
transliteration: nimittāni cha paśhyāmi viparītāni keśhava na cha śhreyo ’nupaśhyāmi hatvā sva-janam āhave

sanskrit: न काङ्क्षे विजयं कृष्ण न च राज्यं सुखानि च। किं नो राज्येन गोविन्द किं भोगैर्जीवितेन वा ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: For I desire not victory, O Krishna, nor kingdom, nor pleasures; what is kingdom to us, O Govinda, what enjoyment or even life?
transliteration: na kāṅkṣhe vijayaṁ kṛiṣhṇa na cha rājyaṁ sukhāni cha kiṁ no rājyena govinda kiṁ bhogair jīvitena vā

sanskrit: येषामर्थे काङ्क्षितं नो राज्यं भोगाः सुखानि च। त इमेऽवस्थिता युद्धे प्राणांस्त्यक्त्वा धनानि च ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Those for whose sake we desire kingdom, enjoyments and pleasures, they stand here in battle, abandoning life and riches—
transliteration: yeṣhām arthe kāṅkṣhitaṁ no rājyaṁ bhogāḥ sukhāni cha ta ime ’vasthitā yuddhe prāṇāṁs tyaktvā dhanāni cha

sanskrit: आचार्याः पितरः पुत्रास्तथैव च पितामहाः। मातुलाः श्चशुराः पौत्राः श्यालाः सम्बन्धिनस्तथा ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Teachers, fathers, sons, as well as grandfathers, mother's brothers, fathers-in-law, grandsons, brothers-in-law, and other relatives.
transliteration: āchāryāḥ pitaraḥ putrās tathaiva cha pitāmahāḥ mātulāḥ śhvaśhurāḥ pautrāḥ śhyālāḥ sambandhinas tathā

sanskrit: एतान्न हन्तुमिच्छामि घ्नतोऽपि मधुसूदन। अपि त्रैलोक्यराज्यस्य हेतोः किं नु महीकृते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: These I do not wish to kill, though myself slain, O Madhusûdana, even for the sake of the kingship of the three worlds; how then for earth?
transliteration: etān na hantum ichchhāmi ghnato ’pi madhusūdana api trailokya-rājyasya hetoḥ kiṁ nu mahī-kṛite

sanskrit: निहत्य धार्तराष्ट्रान्नः का प्रीतिः स्याज्जनार्दन। पापमेवाश्रयेदस्मान्हत्वैतानाततायिनः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Slaying these sons of Dhritarâshtra, what pleasure can be ours, O Janârdana? Killing these desperadoes, sin will but take hold of us.
transliteration: nihatya dhārtarāṣhṭrān naḥ kā prītiḥ syāj janārdana pāpam evāśhrayed asmān hatvaitān ātatāyinaḥ

sanskrit: तस्मान्नार्हा वयं हन्तुं धार्तराष्ट्रान्स्वबान्धवान्। स्वजनं हि कथं हत्वा सुखिनः स्याम माधव ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Therefore we should not kill the sons of Dhritarâshtra, our relatives; for how, killing our kinsmen, may we be happy, O Mâdhava?
transliteration: tasmān nārhā vayaṁ hantuṁ dhārtarāṣhṭrān sa-bāndhavān sva-janaṁ hi kathaṁ hatvā sukhinaḥ syāma mādhava

sanskrit: यद्यप्येते न पश्यन्ति लोभोपहतचेतसः। कुलक्षयकृतं दोषं मित्रद्रोहे च पातकम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Although these, with intelligence overpowered by greed, see no guilt in the destruction of a family, no crime in hostility to friends,
transliteration: yady apy ete na paśhyanti lobhopahata-chetasaḥ kula-kṣhaya-kṛitaṁ doṣhaṁ mitra-drohe cha pātakam

sanskrit: कथं न ज्ञेयमस्माभिः पापादस्मान्निवर्तितुम्। कुलक्षयकृतं दोषं प्रपश्यद्भिर्जनार्दन ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Why should not we learn to turn away from such a sin, O Janârdana, who see the evils in the destruction of a family?
transliteration: kathaṁ na jñeyam asmābhiḥ pāpād asmān nivartitum kula-kṣhaya-kṛitaṁ doṣhaṁ prapaśhyadbhir janārdana

sanskrit: कुलक्षये प्रणश्यन्ति कुलधर्माः सनातनाः। धर्मे नष्टे कुलं कृत्स्नमधर्मोऽभिभवत्युत ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: In the destruction of a family the immemorial family traditions perish; in the perishing of tradition, lawlessness overcomes the whole family;
transliteration: kula-kṣhaye praṇaśhyanti kula-dharmāḥ sanātanāḥ dharme naṣhṭe kulaṁ kṛitsnam adharmo ’bhibhavaty uta

sanskrit: अधर्माभिभवात्कृष्ण प्रदुष्यन्ति कुलस्त्रियः। स्त्रीषु दुष्टासु वार्ष्णेय जायते वर्णसङ्करः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Owing to predominance of lawlessness, O Krishna, the women of the family become corrupt; women corrupted, O Vârshneya, there ariseth caste confusion;
transliteration: adharmābhibhavāt kṛiṣhṇa praduṣhyanti kula-striyaḥ strīṣhu duṣhṭāsu vārṣhṇeya jāyate varṇa-saṅkaraḥ

sanskrit: सङ्करो नरकायैव कुलघ्नानां कुलस्य च। पतन्ति पितरो ह्येषां लुप्तपिण्डोदकक्रियाः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: This confusion draggeth to hell the slayers of the family, and the family; for their ancestors fall, deprived of rice-balls and libations.
transliteration: saṅkaro narakāyaiva kula-ghnānāṁ kulasya cha patanti pitaro hy eṣhāṁ lupta-piṇḍodaka-kriyāḥ

sanskrit: दोषैरेतैः कुलघ्नानां वर्णसङ्करकारकैः। उत्साद्यन्ते जातिधर्माः कुलधर्माश्च शाश्वताः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: By these caste-confusing misdeeds of the slayers of the family, the everlasting caste customs and family customs are abolished.
transliteration: doṣhair etaiḥ kula-ghnānāṁ varṇa-saṅkara-kārakaiḥ utsādyante jāti-dharmāḥ kula-dharmāśh cha śhāśhvatāḥ

sanskrit: उत्सन्नकुलधर्माणां मनुष्याणां जनार्दन। नरकेऽनियतं वासो भवतीत्यनुशुश्रुम ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: The abode of the men whose family customs are extinguished, O Janârdana, is everlastingly in hell. Thus have we heard.
transliteration: utsanna-kula-dharmāṇāṁ manuṣhyāṇāṁ janārdana narake ‘niyataṁ vāso bhavatītyanuśhuśhruma

sanskrit: अहो बत महत्पापं कर्तुं व्यवसिता वयम्। यद्राज्यसुखलोभेन हन्तुं स्वजनमुद्यताः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Alas! in committing a great sin are we engaged, we who are endeavouring to kill our kindred from greed of the pleasures of kingship.
transliteration: aho bata mahat pāpaṁ kartuṁ vyavasitā vayam yad rājya-sukha-lobhena hantuṁ sva-janam udyatāḥ

sanskrit: यदि मामप्रतीकारमशस्त्रं शस्त्रपाणयः। धार्तराष्ट्रा रणे हन्युस्तन्मे क्षेमतरं भवेत् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: If the sons of Dhritarâshtra, weapon in hand, should slay me, unresisting, unarmed, in the battle, that would for me be the better.
transliteration: yadi mām apratīkāram aśhastraṁ śhastra-pāṇayaḥ dhārtarāṣhṭrā raṇe hanyus tan me kṣhemataraṁ bhavet

sanskrit: सञ्जय उवाच एवमुक्त्वाऽर्जुनः संख्ये रथोपस्थ उपाविशत्। विसृज्य सशरं चापं शोकसंविग्नमानसः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Sanjaya said: Having thus spoken on the battle-field, Arjuna sank down on the seat of the chariot, casting away his bow and arrow, his mind overborne by grief.
transliteration: sañjaya uvācha evam uktvārjunaḥ saṅkhye rathopastha upāviśhat visṛijya sa-śharaṁ chāpaṁ śhoka-saṁvigna-mānasaḥ

sanskrit: सञ्जय उवाच तं तथा कृपयाऽविष्टमश्रुपूर्णाकुलेक्षणम्। विषीदन्तमिदं वाक्यमुवाच मधुसूदनः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Sanjaya said: To him thus with pity overcome, with smarting brimming eyes, despondent, Madhusûdana spake these words:
transliteration: sañjaya uvācha taṁ tathā kṛipayāviṣhṭamaśhru pūrṇākulekṣhaṇam viṣhīdantamidaṁ vākyam uvācha madhusūdanaḥ

sanskrit: श्री भगवानुवाच कुतस्त्वा कश्मलमिदं विषमे समुपस्थितम्। अनार्यजुष्टमस्वर्ग्यमकीर्तिकरमर्जुन ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: The Blessed Lord said: Whence hath this dejection befallen thee in this perilous strait, ignoble, heaven-closing, infamous, O Arjuna?
transliteration: śhrī bhagavān uvācha kutastvā kaśhmalamidaṁ viṣhame samupasthitam anārya-juṣhṭamaswargyam akīrti-karam arjuna

sanskrit: क्लैब्यं मा स्म गमः पार्थ नैतत्त्वय्युपपद्यते। क्षुद्रं हृदयदौर्बल्यं त्यक्त्वोत्तिष्ठ परन्तप ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Yield not to impotence, O Partha! it doth not befit thee. Shake off this paltry faint-heartedness! Stand up, Parantapa!
transliteration: klaibyaṁ mā sma gamaḥ pārtha naitat tvayyupapadyate kṣhudraṁ hṛidaya-daurbalyaṁ tyaktvottiṣhṭha parantapa

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच कथं भीष्ममहं संख्ये द्रोणं च मधुसूदन। इषुभिः प्रतियोत्स्यामि पूजार्हावरिसूदन ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Arjuna said: How, O Madhusûdana, shall I attack Bhîshma and Drona with arrows in battle, they who are worthy of reverence, O slayer of foes?
transliteration: arjuna uvācha kathaṁ bhīṣhmam ahaṁ sankhye droṇaṁ cha madhusūdana iṣhubhiḥ pratiyotsyāmi pūjārhāvari-sūdana

sanskrit: गुरूनहत्वा हि महानुभावान् श्रेयो भोक्तुं भैक्ष्यमपीह लोके। हत्वार्थकामांस्तु गुरूनिहैव भुञ्जीय भोगान् रुधिरप्रदिग्धान् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Better in this world to eat even the beggar's crust than to slay these most noble Gurus. Slaying these Gurus, our well-wishers, I should taste of blood-besprinkled feasts.
transliteration: gurūnahatvā hi mahānubhāvān śhreyo bhoktuṁ bhaikṣhyamapīha loke hatvārtha-kāmāṁstu gurūnihaiva bhuñjīya bhogān rudhira-pradigdhān

sanskrit: न चैतद्विद्मः कतरन्नो गरीयो यद्वा जयेम यदि वा नो जयेयुः। यानेव हत्वा न जिजीविषाम स्तेऽवस्थिताः प्रमुखे धार्तराष्ट्राः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Nor know I which for us be the better, that we conquer them or they conquer us—these, whom having slain we should not care to live, even these arrayed against us, the sons of Dhritarâshtra.
transliteration: na chaitadvidmaḥ kataranno garīyo yadvā jayema yadi vā no jayeyuḥ yāneva hatvā na jijīviṣhāmas te ’vasthitāḥ pramukhe dhārtarāṣhṭrāḥ

sanskrit: कार्पण्यदोषोपहतस्वभावः पृच्छामि त्वां धर्मसंमूढचेताः। यच्छ्रेयः स्यान्निश्िचतं ब्रूहि तन्मे शिष्यस्तेऽहं शाधि मां त्वां प्रपन्नम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: My heart is weighed down with the vice of faintness; my mind is confused as to duty. I ask thee which may be the better—that tell me decisively. I am thy disciple, suppliant to Thee; teach me.
transliteration: kārpaṇya-doṣhopahata-svabhāvaḥ pṛichchhāmi tvāṁ dharma-sammūḍha-chetāḥ yach-chhreyaḥ syānniśhchitaṁ brūhi tanme śhiṣhyaste ’haṁ śhādhi māṁ tvāṁ prapannam

sanskrit: न हि प्रपश्यामि ममापनुद्या द्यच्छोकमुच्छोषणमिन्द्रियाणाम्। अवाप्य भूमावसपत्नमृद्धम् राज्यं सुराणामपि चाधिपत्यम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: For I see not that it would drive away this anguish that withers up my senses, if I should attain unrivalled monarchy on earth, or even the sovereignty of the Shining Ones.
transliteration: na hi prapaśhyāmi mamāpanudyād yach-chhokam uchchhoṣhaṇam-indriyāṇām avāpya bhūmāv-asapatnamṛiddhaṁ rājyaṁ surāṇāmapi chādhipatyam

sanskrit: सञ्जय उवाच एवमुक्त्वा हृषीकेशं गुडाकेशः परन्तप। न योत्स्य इति गोविन्दमुक्त्वा तूष्णीं बभूव ह ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Sanjaya said: Gudâkesha, conqueror of his foes, having thus addressed Hrishîkesha and said to Govinda, "I will not fight!", became silent.
transliteration: sañjaya uvācha evam-uktvā hṛiṣhīkeśhaṁ guḍākeśhaḥ parantapa na yotsya iti govindam uktvā tūṣhṇīṁ babhūva ha

sanskrit: तमुवाच हृषीकेशः प्रहसन्निव भारत। सेनयोरुभयोर्मध्ये विषीदन्तमिदं वचः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Then Hrishîkesha, smiling, as it were, O Bhârata, spake these words to him, despondent, in the midst of the two armies:
transliteration: tam-uvācha hṛiṣhīkeśhaḥ prahasanniva bhārata senayorubhayor-madhye viṣhīdantam-idaṁ vachaḥ

sanskrit: श्री भगवानुवाच अशोच्यानन्वशोचस्त्वं प्रज्ञावादांश्च भाषसे। गतासूनगतासूंश्च नानुशोचन्ति पण्डिताः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: The Blessed Lord said: Thou grievest for those that should not be griev ed for, yet speakest words of wisdom. The wise grieve neither for the living nor for the dead.
transliteration: śhrī bhagavān uvācha aśhochyān-anvaśhochas-tvaṁ prajñā-vādānśh cha bhāṣhase gatāsūn-agatāsūnśh-cha nānuśhochanti paṇḍitāḥ

sanskrit: न त्वेवाहं जातु नासं न त्वं नेमे जनाधिपाः। न चैव न भविष्यामः सर्वे वयमतः परम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Nor at any time verily was I not, nor thou, nor these princes of men, nor verily shall we ever cease to be, hereafter.
transliteration: na tvevāhaṁ jātu nāsaṁ na tvaṁ neme janādhipāḥ na chaiva na bhaviṣhyāmaḥ sarve vayamataḥ param

sanskrit: देहिनोऽस्मिन्यथा देहे कौमारं यौवनं जरा। तथा देहान्तरप्राप्तिर्धीरस्तत्र न मुह्यति ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: As the dweller in the body experienceth in the body childhood, youth, old age, so passeth he on to another body; the steadfast one grieveth not thereat.
transliteration: dehino ’smin yathā dehe kaumāraṁ yauvanaṁ jarā tathā dehāntara-prāptir dhīras tatra na muhyati

sanskrit: मात्रास्पर्शास्तु कौन्तेय शीतोष्णसुखदुःखदाः। आगमापायिनोऽनित्यास्तांस्तितिक्षस्व भारत ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: The contacts of matter, O son of Kuntî, giving cold and heat, pleasure and pain, they come and go, impermanent; endure them bravely, O Bhârata.
transliteration: mātrā-sparśhās tu kaunteya śhītoṣhṇa-sukha-duḥkha-dāḥ āgamāpāyino ’nityās tans-titikṣhasva bhārata

sanskrit: यं हि न व्यथयन्त्येते पुरुषं पुरुषर्षभ। समदुःखसुखं धीरं सोऽमृतत्वाय कल्पते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: The man whom these torment not, O chief of men, balanced in pain and pleasure, steadfast, he is fitted for immortality.
transliteration: yaṁ hi na vyathayantyete puruṣhaṁ puruṣharṣhabha sama-duḥkha-sukhaṁ dhīraṁ so ’mṛitatvāya kalpate

sanskrit: नासतो विद्यते भावो नाभावो विद्यते सतः। उभयोरपि दृष्टोऽन्तस्त्वनयोस्तत्त्वदर्शिभिः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: The unreal hath no being; the real never ceaseth to be; the truth about both hath been perceived by the seers of the essence of things.
transliteration: nāsato vidyate bhāvo nābhāvo vidyate sataḥ ubhayorapi dṛiṣhṭo ’nta stvanayos tattva-darśhibhiḥ

sanskrit: अविनाशि तु तद्विद्धि येन सर्वमिदं ततम्। विनाशमव्ययस्यास्य न कश्चित् कर्तुमर्हति ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Know That to be indestructible by whom all this is pervaded. Nor can any work the destruction of that imperishable One.
transliteration: avināśhi tu tadviddhi yena sarvam idaṁ tatam vināśham avyayasyāsya na kaśhchit kartum arhati

sanskrit: अन्तवन्त इमे देहा नित्यस्योक्ताः शरीरिणः। अनाशिनोऽप्रमेयस्य तस्माद्युध्यस्व भारत ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: These bodies of the embodied One, who is eternal, indestructible and immeasurable, are known as finite. Therefore fight, O Bhârata.
transliteration: antavanta ime dehā nityasyoktāḥ śharīriṇaḥ anāśhino ’prameyasya tasmād yudhyasva bhārata

sanskrit: य एनं वेत्ति हन्तारं यश्चैनं मन्यते हतम्। उभौ तौ न विजानीतो नायं हन्ति न हन्यते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: He who regardeth this as a slayer, and he who thinketh he is slain, both of them are ignorant. He slayeth not, nor is he slain.
transliteration: ya enaṁ vetti hantāraṁ yaśh chainaṁ manyate hatam ubhau tau na vijānīto nāyaṁ hanti na hanyate

sanskrit: न जायते म्रियते वा कदाचि न्नायं भूत्वा भविता वा न भूयः। अजो नित्यः शाश्वतोऽयं पुराणो न हन्यते हन्यमाने शरीरे ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: He is not born, nor doth he die; nor having been, ceaseth he any more to be; unborn, perpetual, eternal and ancient, he is not slain when the body is slaughtered.
transliteration: na jāyate mriyate vā kadāchin nāyaṁ bhūtvā bhavitā vā na bhūyaḥ ajo nityaḥ śhāśhvato ’yaṁ purāṇo na hanyate hanyamāne śharīre

sanskrit: वेदाविनाशिनं नित्यं य एनमजमव्ययम्। कथं स पुरुषः पार्थ कं घातयति हन्ति कम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Who knoweth him indestructible, perpetual, unborn, undiminishing, how can that man slay, O Pârtha, or cause to be slain?
transliteration: vedāvināśhinaṁ nityaṁ ya enam ajam avyayam kathaṁ sa puruṣhaḥ pārtha kaṁ ghātayati hanti kam

sanskrit: वासांसि जीर्णानि यथा विहाय नवानि गृह्णाति नरोऽपराणि। तथा शरीराणि विहाय जीर्णा न्यन्यानि संयाति नवानि देही ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: As a man, casting off worn-out garments, taketh new ones, so the dweller in the body, casting off worn-out bodies, entereth into others that are new.
transliteration: vāsānsi jīrṇāni yathā vihāya navāni gṛihṇāti naro ’parāṇi tathā śharīrāṇi vihāya jīrṇānya nyāni sanyāti navāni dehī

sanskrit: नैनं छिन्दन्ति शस्त्राणि नैनं दहति पावकः। न चैनं क्लेदयन्त्यापो न शोषयति मारुतः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Weapons cleave him not, nor fire burneth him, nor waters wet him, nor wind drieth him away.
transliteration: nainaṁ chhindanti śhastrāṇi nainaṁ dahati pāvakaḥ na chainaṁ kledayantyāpo na śhoṣhayati mārutaḥ

sanskrit: अच्छेद्योऽयमदाह्योऽयमक्लेद्योऽशोष्य एव च। नित्यः सर्वगतः स्थाणुरचलोऽयं सनातनः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Uncleavable he, incombustible he, and indeed neither to be wetted nor dried away; perpetual, all-pervasive, stable, immovable, ancient.
transliteration: achchhedyo ’yam adāhyo ’yam akledyo ’śhoṣhya eva cha nityaḥ sarva-gataḥ sthāṇur achalo ’yaṁ sanātanaḥ

sanskrit: अव्यक्तोऽयमचिन्त्योऽयमविकार्योऽयमुच्यते। तस्मादेवं विदित्वैनं नानुशोचितुमर्हसि ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Unmanifest, unthinkable, immutable, he is called; therefore knowing him as such, thou shouldst not grieve.
transliteration: avyakto ’yam achintyo ’yam avikāryo ’yam uchyate tasmādevaṁ viditvainaṁ nānuśhochitum arhasi

sanskrit: अथ चैनं नित्यजातं नित्यं वा मन्यसे मृतम्। तथापि त्वं महाबाहो नैवं शोचितुमर्हसि ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Or if thou thinkest of him as being constantly born and constantly dying, even then, O mighty-armed, thou shouldst not grieve.
transliteration: atha chainaṁ nitya-jātaṁ nityaṁ vā manyase mṛitam tathāpi tvaṁ mahā-bāho naivaṁ śhochitum arhasi

sanskrit: जातस्य हि ध्रुवो मृत्युर्ध्रुवं जन्म मृतस्य च। तस्मादपरिहार्येऽर्थे न त्वं शोचितुमर्हसि ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: For certain is death for the born, and certain is birth for the dead; therefore over the inevitable thou shouldst not grieve.
transliteration: jātasya hi dhruvo mṛityur dhruvaṁ janma mṛitasya cha tasmād aparihārye ’rthe na tvaṁ śhochitum arhasi

sanskrit: अव्यक्तादीनि भूतानि व्यक्तमध्यानि भारत। अव्यक्तनिधनान्येव तत्र का परिदेवना ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Beings are unmanifest in their origin, manifest in their midmost state, O Bhârata, unmanifest likewise are they in dissolution. What room then for lamentation?
transliteration: avyaktādīni bhūtāni vyakta-madhyāni bhārata avyakta-nidhanānyeva tatra kā paridevanā

sanskrit: आश्चर्यवत्पश्यति कश्चिदेन माश्चर्यवद्वदति तथैव चान्यः। आश्चर्यवच्चैनमन्यः श्रृणोति श्रुत्वाप्येनं वेद न चैव कश्चित् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: As marvellous one regardeth him; as marvellous another speaketh thereof; as marvellous another heareth thereof; yet having heard none indeed understandeth.
transliteration: āśhcharya-vat paśhyati kaśhchid enan āśhcharya-vad vadati tathaiva chānyaḥ āśhcharya-vach chainam anyaḥ śhṛiṇoti śhrutvāpyenaṁ veda na chaiva kaśhchit

sanskrit: देही नित्यमवध्योऽयं देहे सर्वस्य भारत। तस्मात्सर्वाणि भूतानि न त्वं शोचितुमर्हसि ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: This dweller in the body of everyone is ever invulnerable, O Bhârata; therefore thou shouldst not grieve for any creature.
transliteration: dehī nityam avadhyo ’yaṁ dehe sarvasya bhārata tasmāt sarvāṇi bhūtāni na tvaṁ śhochitum arhasi

sanskrit: स्वधर्ममपि चावेक्ष्य न विकम्पितुमर्हसि। धर्म्याद्धि युद्धाछ्रेयोऽन्यत्क्षत्रियस्य न विद्यते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Further, looking to thine own duty thou shouldst not tremble; for there is nothing more welcome to a Kshattriya than righteous war.
transliteration: swa-dharmam api chāvekṣhya na vikampitum arhasi dharmyāddhi yuddhāch chhreyo ’nyat kṣhatriyasya na vidyate

sanskrit: यदृच्छया चोपपन्नं स्वर्गद्वारमपावृतम्। सुखिनः क्षत्रियाः पार्थ लभन्ते युद्धमीदृशम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Happy the Kshattriyas, O Pârtha, who obtain such a fight, offered unsought as an open door to heaven.
transliteration: yadṛichchhayā chopapannaṁ swarga-dvāram apāvṛitam sukhinaḥ kṣhatriyāḥ pārtha labhante yuddham īdṛiśham

sanskrit: अथ चैत्त्वमिमं धर्म्यं संग्रामं न करिष्यसि। ततः स्वधर्मं कीर्तिं च हित्वा पापमवाप्स्यसि ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: But if thou wilt not carry on this righteous warfare, then casting away thine own duty and thine honour, thou wilt incur sin.
transliteration: atha chet tvam imaṁ dharmyaṁ saṅgrāmaṁ na kariṣhyasi tataḥ sva-dharmaṁ kīrtiṁ cha hitvā pāpam avāpsyasi

sanskrit: अकीर्तिं चापि भूतानि कथयिष्यन्ति तेऽव्ययाम्। संभावितस्य चाकीर्तिर्मरणादतिरिच्यते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Men will recount thy perpetual dishonour, and, to one highly esteemed, dishonour exceedeth death.
transliteration: akīrtiṁ chāpi bhūtāni kathayiṣhyanti te ’vyayām sambhāvitasya chākīrtir maraṇād atirichyate

sanskrit: भयाद्रणादुपरतं मंस्यन्ते त्वां महारथाः। येषां च त्वं बहुमतो भूत्वा यास्यसि लाघवम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: The great car-warriors will think thee fled from the battle from fear, and thou, that wast highly thought of by them, wilt be lightly held.
transliteration: bhayād raṇād uparataṁ mansyante tvāṁ mahā-rathāḥ yeṣhāṁ cha tvaṁ bahu-mato bhūtvā yāsyasi lāghavam

sanskrit: अवाच्यवादांश्च बहून् वदिष्यन्ति तवाहिताः। निन्दन्तस्तव सामर्थ्यं ततो दुःखतरं नु किम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Many unseemly words will be spoken by thine enemies, slandering thy strength; what more painful than that?
transliteration: avāchya-vādānśh cha bahūn vadiṣhyanti tavāhitāḥ nindantastava sāmarthyaṁ tato duḥkhataraṁ nu kim

sanskrit: हतो वा प्राप्स्यसि स्वर्गं जित्वा वा भोक्ष्यसे महीम्। तस्मादुत्तिष्ठ कौन्तेय युद्धाय कृतनिश्चयः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Slain, thou wilt obtain heaven; victorious, thou wilt enjoy the earth; therefore stand up, O son of Kuntî, resolute to fight.
transliteration: hato vā prāpsyasi swargaṁ jitvā vā bhokṣhyase mahīm tasmād uttiṣhṭha kaunteya yuddhāya kṛita-niśhchayaḥ

sanskrit: सुखदुःखे समे कृत्वा लाभालाभौ जयाजयौ। ततो युद्धाय युज्यस्व नैवं पापमवाप्स्यसि ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Taking as equal pleasure and pain, gain and loss, victory and defeat, gird thee for the battle; thus thou shalt not incur sin.
transliteration: sukha-duḥkhe same kṛitvā lābhālābhau jayājayau tato yuddhāya yujyasva naivaṁ pāpam avāpsyasi

sanskrit: एषा तेऽभिहिता सांख्ये बुद्धिर्योगे त्विमां श्रृणु। बुद्ध्यायुक्तो यया पार्थ कर्मबन्धं प्रहास्यसि ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: This teaching set forth to thee is in accordance with the Sâñkhya ; hear it now according to the Yoga, imbued with which teaching, O Pârtha, thou shalt cast away the bonds of action.
transliteration: eṣhā te ’bhihitā sānkhye buddhir yoge tvimāṁ śhṛiṇu buddhyā yukto yayā pārtha karma-bandhaṁ prahāsyasi

sanskrit: नेहाभिक्रमनाशोऽस्ति प्रत्यवायो न विद्यते। स्वल्पमप्यस्य धर्मस्य त्रायते महतो भयात् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: In this there is no loss of effort, nor is there transgression. Even a little of this knowledge protects from great fear.
transliteration: nehābhikrama-nāśho ’sti pratyavāyo na vidyate svalpam apyasya dharmasya trāyate mahato bhayāt

sanskrit: व्यवसायात्मिका बुद्धिरेकेह कुरुनन्दन। बहुशाखा ह्यनन्ताश्च बुद्धयोऽव्यवसायिनाम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: The determinate Reason is but one-pointed, O joy of the Kurus; many-branched and endless are the thoughts of the irresolute.
transliteration: vyavasāyātmikā buddhir ekeha kuru-nandana bahu-śhākhā hyanantāśh cha buddhayo ’vyavasāyinām

sanskrit: यामिमां पुष्पितां वाचं प्रवदन्त्यविपश्चितः। वेदवादरताः पार्थ नान्यदस्तीति वादिनः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Flowery speech is uttered by the foolish, rejoicing in the letter of the Vedas, O Pârtha, saying: "There is naught but this";
transliteration: yāmimāṁ puṣhpitāṁ vāchaṁ pravadanty-avipaśhchitaḥ veda-vāda-ratāḥ pārtha nānyad astīti vādinaḥ kāmātmānaḥ swarga-parā janma-karma-phala-pradām kriyā-viśheṣha-bahulāṁ bhogaiśhwarya-gatiṁ prati

sanskrit: कामात्मानः स्वर्गपरा जन्मकर्मफलप्रदाम्। क्रियाविशेषबहुलां भोगैश्वर्यगतिं प्रति ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: With desire for self, with heaven for goal, they offer birth as the fruit of action, and prescribe many and various ceremonies for the attainment of pleasure and lordship.
transliteration: kāmātmānaḥ svarga-parā janma-karma-phala-pradām kriyā-viśeṣa-bahulāṁ bhogaiśvarya-gatiṁ prati

sanskrit: भोगैश्वर्यप्रसक्तानां तयापहृतचेतसाम्। व्यवसायात्मिका बुद्धिः समाधौ न विधीयते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: For them who cling to pleasure and lordship, whose minds are captivated by such teaching, is not designed this determinate Reason, on contemplation steadily bent.
transliteration: bhogaiśwvarya-prasaktānāṁ tayāpahṛita-chetasām vyavasāyātmikā buddhiḥ samādhau na vidhīyate

sanskrit: त्रैगुण्यविषया वेदा निस्त्रैगुण्यो भवार्जुन। निर्द्वन्द्वो नित्यसत्त्वस्थो निर्योगक्षेम आत्मवान् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: The Vedas deal with the three attributes; be thou above these three attributes, O Arjuna; beyond the pairs of opposites, ever steadfast in purity, careless of possessions, full of the Self .
transliteration: trai-guṇya-viṣhayā vedā nistrai-guṇyo bhavārjuna nirdvandvo nitya-sattva-stho niryoga-kṣhema ātmavān

sanskrit: यावानर्थ उदपाने सर्वतः संप्लुतोदके। तावान्सर्वेषु वेदेषु ब्राह्मणस्य विजानतः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: All the Vedas are as useful to an enlightened Brâhmana as is a tank in a place covered all over with water.
transliteration: yāvān artha udapāne sarvataḥ samplutodake tāvānsarveṣhu vedeṣhu brāhmaṇasya vijānataḥ

sanskrit: कर्मण्येवाधिकारस्ते मा फलेषु कदाचन। मा कर्मफलहेतुर्भूर्मा ते सङ्गोऽस्त्वकर्मणि ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Thy business is with the action only, never with its fruits; so let not the fruit of action be thy motive, nor be thou to inaction attached.
transliteration: karmaṇy-evādhikāras te mā phaleṣhu kadāchana mā karma-phala-hetur bhūr mā te saṅgo ’stvakarmaṇi

sanskrit: योगस्थः कुरु कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा धनञ्जय। सिद्ध्यसिद्ध्योः समो भूत्वा समत्वं योग उच्यते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Perform action, O Dhananjaya, dwelling in union with the divine, renouncing attachments and balanced evenly in success and failure: equilibrium is called yoga.
transliteration: yoga-sthaḥ kuru karmāṇi saṅgaṁ tyaktvā dhanañjaya siddhy-asiddhyoḥ samo bhūtvā samatvaṁ yoga uchyate

sanskrit: दूरेण ह्यवरं कर्म बुद्धियोगाद्धनञ्जय। बुद्धौ शरणमन्विच्छ कृपणाः फलहेतवः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Far lower than the Yoga of Discrimination is action, O Dhananjaya. Take thou refuge in the Pure Reason; pitiable are they who work for fruit.
transliteration: dūreṇa hy-avaraṁ karma buddhi-yogād dhanañjaya buddhau śharaṇam anvichchha kṛipaṇāḥ phala-hetavaḥ

sanskrit: बुद्धियुक्तो जहातीह उभे सुकृतदुष्कृते। तस्माद्योगाय युज्यस्व योगः कर्मसु कौशलम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: United to the Pure Reason one abandoneth here both good and evil deeds; therefore cleave thou to yoga; yoga is skill in action.
transliteration: buddhi-yukto jahātīha ubhe sukṛita-duṣhkṛite tasmād yogāya yujyasva yogaḥ karmasu kauśhalam

sanskrit: कर्मजं बुद्धियुक्ता हि फलं त्यक्त्वा मनीषिणः। जन्मबन्धविनिर्मुक्ताः पदं गच्छन्त्यनामयम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: The Sages, united to the Pure Reason, renounce the fruit which action yieldeth, and, liberated from the bonds of birth, they go to the blissful seat.
transliteration: karma-jaṁ buddhi-yuktā hi phalaṁ tyaktvā manīṣhiṇaḥ janma-bandha-vinirmuktāḥ padaṁ gachchhanty-anāmayam

sanskrit: यदा ते मोहकलिलं बुद्धिर्व्यतितरिष्यति। तदा गन्तासि निर्वेदं श्रोतव्यस्य श्रुतस्य च ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: When thy mind shall escape from this tangle of delusion, then thou shalt rise to indifference as to what has been heard and shall be heard.
transliteration: yadā te moha-kalilaṁ buddhir vyatitariṣhyati tadā gantāsi nirvedaṁ śhrotavyasya śhrutasya cha

sanskrit: श्रुतिविप्रतिपन्ना ते यदा स्थास्यति निश्चला। समाधावचला बुद्धिस्तदा योगमवाप्स्यसि ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: When thy mind, bewildered by the Scriptures, shall stand immovable, fixed in contemplation, then shalt thou attain unto yoga.
transliteration: śhruti-vipratipannā te yadā sthāsyati niśhchalā samādhāv-achalā buddhis tadā yogam avāpsyasi

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच स्थितप्रज्ञस्य का भाषा समाधिस्थस्य केशव। स्थितधीः किं प्रभाषेत किमासीत व्रजेत किम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Arjuna said: What the mark, of him who is stable of mind, steadfast in contemplation, O Keshava? How doth the stable-minded talk, how doth he sit, how walk?
transliteration: arjuna uvācha sthita-prajñasya kā bhāṣhā samādhi-sthasya keśhava sthita-dhīḥ kiṁ prabhāṣheta kim āsīta vrajeta kim

sanskrit: श्री भगवानुवाच प्रजहाति यदा कामान् सर्वान् पार्थ मनोगतान्। आत्मन्येवात्मना तुष्टः स्थितप्रज्ञस्तदोच्यते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: The Blessed Lord said: When a man abandoneth, O Pârtha, all the desires of the heart, and is satisfied in the Self by the Self , then is he called stable in mind.
transliteration: śhrī bhagavān uvācha prajahāti yadā kāmān sarvān pārtha mano-gatān ātmany-evātmanā tuṣhṭaḥ sthita-prajñas tadochyate

sanskrit: दुःखेष्वनुद्विग्नमनाः सुखेषु विगतस्पृहः। वीतरागभयक्रोधः स्थितधीर्मुनिरुच्यते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: He whose mind is free from anxiety amid pains, indifferent amid pleasures, loosed from passion, fear and anger, is called a sage of stable mind.
transliteration: duḥkheṣhv-anudvigna-manāḥ sukheṣhu vigata-spṛihaḥ vīta-rāga-bhaya-krodhaḥ sthita-dhīr munir uchyate

sanskrit: यः सर्वत्रानभिस्नेहस्तत्तत्प्राप्य शुभाशुभम्। नाभिनन्दति न द्वेष्टि तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: He who on every side is without attachments, whatever hap of fair and foul, who neither likes nor dislikes, of such a one the understanding is well poised.
transliteration: yaḥ sarvatrānabhisnehas tat tat prāpya śhubhāśhubham nābhinandati na dveṣhṭi tasya prajñā pratiṣhṭhitā

sanskrit: यदा संहरते चायं कूर्मोऽङ्गानीव सर्वशः। इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेभ्यस्तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: When, again, as a tortoise draws in on all sides its limbs, he withdraws his senses from the objects of sense, then is his understanding well poised.
transliteration: yadā sanharate chāyaṁ kūrmo ’ṅgānīva sarvaśhaḥ indriyāṇīndriyārthebhyas tasya prajñā pratiṣhṭhitā

sanskrit: विषया विनिवर्तन्ते निराहारस्य देहिनः। रसवर्जं रसोऽप्यस्य परं दृष्ट्वा निवर्तते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: The objects of sense, but not the relish for them, turn away from an abstemious dweller in the body; and even relish turneth away from him after the Supreme is seen.
transliteration: viṣhayā vinivartante nirāhārasya dehinaḥ rasa-varjaṁ raso ’pyasya paraṁ dṛiṣhṭvā nivartate

sanskrit: यततो ह्यपि कौन्तेय पुरुषस्य विपश्चितः। इन्द्रियाणि प्रमाथीनि हरन्ति प्रसभं मनः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: O son of Kuntî, the excited senses of even a wise man, though he be striving, impetuously carry away his mind.
transliteration: yatato hyapi kaunteya puruṣhasya vipaśhchitaḥ indriyāṇi pramāthīni haranti prasabhaṁ manaḥ

sanskrit: तानि सर्वाणि संयम्य युक्त आसीत मत्परः। वशे हि यस्येन्द्रियाणि तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Having restrained them all, he should sit harmonised, I his supreme goal; for, whose senses are mastered, of him the understanding is well poised.
transliteration: tāni sarvāṇi sanyamya yukta āsīta mat-paraḥ vaśhe hi yasyendriyāṇi tasya prajñā pratiṣhṭhitā

sanskrit: ध्यायतो विषयान्पुंसः सङ्गस्तेषूपजायते। सङ्गात् संजायते कामः कामात्क्रोधोऽभिजायते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Man, musing on the objects of sense, conceiveth an attachment to these; from attachment ariseth desire; from desire anger cometh forth;
transliteration: dhyāyato viṣhayān puṁsaḥ saṅgas teṣhūpajāyate saṅgāt sañjāyate kāmaḥ kāmāt krodho ’bhijāyate

sanskrit: क्रोधाद्भवति संमोहः संमोहात्स्मृतिविभ्रमः। स्मृतिभ्रंशाद् बुद्धिनाशो बुद्धिनाशात्प्रणश्यति ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: From anger proceedeth delusion; from delusion confused memory; from confused memory the destruction of Reason; from destruction of Reason he perishes.
transliteration: krodhād bhavati sammohaḥ sammohāt smṛiti-vibhramaḥ smṛiti-bhranśhād buddhi-nāśho buddhi-nāśhāt praṇaśhyati

sanskrit: रागद्वेषवियुक्तैस्तु विषयानिन्द्रियैश्चरन्। आत्मवश्यैर्विधेयात्मा प्रसादमधिगच्छति ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: But the disciplined self, moving among sense-objects with senses free from attraction and repulsion, mastered by the Self, goeth to peace.
transliteration: rāga-dveṣha-viyuktais tu viṣhayān indriyaiśh charan ātma-vaśhyair-vidheyātmā prasādam adhigachchhati

sanskrit: प्रसादे सर्वदुःखानां हानिरस्योपजायते। प्रसन्नचेतसो ह्याशु बुद्धिः पर्यवतिष्ठते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: In that Peace the extinction of all pains ariseth for him, for of him whose heart is peaceful the Reason soon attaineth equilibrium.
transliteration: prasāde sarva-duḥkhānāṁ hānir asyopajāyate prasanna-chetaso hyāśhu buddhiḥ paryavatiṣhṭhate

sanskrit: नास्ति बुद्धिरयुक्तस्य न चायुक्तस्य भावना। न चाभावयतः शान्तिरशान्तस्य कुतः सुखम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: There is no Pure Reason for the non-harmonised, nor for the non-harmonised is there concentration; for him without concentration there is no peace, and for the unpeaceful how can there be happiness?
transliteration: nāsti buddhir-ayuktasya na chāyuktasya bhāvanā na chābhāvayataḥ śhāntir aśhāntasya kutaḥ sukham

sanskrit: इन्द्रियाणां हि चरतां यन्मनोऽनुविधीयते। तदस्य हरति प्रज्ञां वायुर्नावमिवाम्भसि ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Such of the roving senses as the mind yieldeth to, that hurries away the understanding, just as the gale hurries away a ship upon the waters.
transliteration: indriyāṇāṁ hi charatāṁ yan mano ’nuvidhīyate tadasya harati prajñāṁ vāyur nāvam ivāmbhasi

sanskrit: तस्माद्यस्य महाबाहो निगृहीतानि सर्वशः। इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेभ्यस्तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Therefore, O mighty-armed, whose senses are all completely restrained from the objects of sense, of him the understanding is well poised.
transliteration: tasmād yasya mahā-bāho nigṛihītāni sarvaśhaḥ indriyāṇīndriyārthebhyas tasya prajñā pratiṣhṭhitā

sanskrit: या निशा सर्वभूतानां तस्यां जागर्ति संयमी। यस्यां जाग्रति भूतानि सा निशा पश्यतो मुनेः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: That which is the night of all beings, for the disciplined man is the time of waking; when other beings are waking, then is it night for the sage who seeth.
transliteration: yā niśhā sarva-bhūtānāṁ tasyāṁ jāgarti sanyamī yasyāṁ jāgrati bhūtāni sā niśhā paśhyato muneḥ

sanskrit: आपूर्यमाणमचलप्रतिष्ठं समुद्रमापः प्रविशन्ति यद्वत्। तद्वत्कामा यं प्रविशन्ति सर्वे स शान्तिमाप्नोति न कामकामी ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: He attaineth Peace, into whom all desires flow as rivers flow into the ocean, which is filled with water, but remaineth unmoved—not he who desireth desires.
transliteration: āpūryamāṇam achala-pratiṣhṭhaṁ samudram āpaḥ praviśhanti yadvat tadvat kāmā yaṁ praviśhanti sarve sa śhāntim āpnoti na kāma-kāmī

sanskrit: विहाय कामान्यः सर्वान्पुमांश्चरति निःस्पृहः। निर्ममो निरहंकारः स शांतिमधिगच्छति ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Whoso forsaketh all desires and goeth onwards free from yearnings, selfless and without egoism—he goeth to Peace.
transliteration: vihāya kāmān yaḥ sarvān pumānśh charati niḥspṛihaḥ nirmamo nirahankāraḥ sa śhāntim adhigachchhati

sanskrit: एषा ब्राह्मी स्थितिः पार्थ नैनां प्राप्य विमुह्यति। स्थित्वाऽस्यामन्तकालेऽपि ब्रह्मनिर्वाणमृच्छति ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: This is the Eternal state, O son of Prithâ. Having attained thereto, none is bewildered. Who, even at the death-hour, is established therein, he goeth to the Nirvâna of the Eternal .
transliteration: eṣhā brāhmī sthitiḥ pārtha naināṁ prāpya vimuhyati sthitvāsyām anta-kāle ’pi brahma-nirvāṇam ṛichchhati

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच ज्यायसी चेत्कर्मणस्ते मता बुद्धिर्जनार्दन। तत्किं कर्मणि घोरे मां नियोजयसि केशव ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Arjuna said: If it be thought by Thee that knowledge is superior to action, O Janârdana, why dost Thou, O Keshava, enjoin on me this terrible action?
transliteration: arjuna uvācha jyāyasī chet karmaṇas te matā buddhir janārdana tat kiṁ karmaṇi ghore māṁ niyojayasi keśhava

sanskrit: व्यामिश्रेणेव वाक्येन बुद्धिं मोहयसीव मे। तदेकं वद निश्िचत्य येन श्रेयोऽहमाप्नुयाम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: With these perplexing words Thou only confusest my understanding; therefore tell me with certainty the one way by which I may reach bliss.
transliteration: vyāmiśhreṇeva vākyena buddhiṁ mohayasīva me tad ekaṁ vada niśhchitya yena śhreyo ’ham āpnuyām

sanskrit: श्री भगवानुवाच लोकेऽस्मिन्द्विविधा निष्ठा पुरा प्रोक्ता मयानघ। ज्ञानयोगेन सांख्यानां कर्मयोगेन योगिनाम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: The Blessed Lord said: In this world there is a twofold path, as I before said, O sinless one: that of yoga by knowledge, of the Sâñkhyas; and that of yoga by action, of the Yogis.
transliteration: śhrī bhagavān uvācha loke’smin dvi-vidhā niṣhṭhā purā proktā mayānagha jñāna-yogena sāṅkhyānāṁ karma-yogena yoginām

sanskrit: न कर्मणामनारम्भान्नैष्कर्म्यं पुरुषोऽश्नुते। न च संन्यसनादेव सिद्धिं समधिगच्छति ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Man winneth not freedom from action by abstaining from activity, nor by mere renunciation doth he rise to perfection.
transliteration: na karmaṇām anārambhān naiṣhkarmyaṁ puruṣho ’śhnute na cha sannyasanād eva siddhiṁ samadhigachchhati

sanskrit: न हि कश्िचत्क्षणमपि जातु तिष्ठत्यकर्मकृत्। कार्यते ह्यवशः कर्म सर्वः प्रकृतिजैर्गुणैः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Nor can anyone, even for an instant, remain really actionless; for helplessly is everyone driven to action by the qualities born of nature.
transliteration: na hi kaśhchit kṣhaṇam api jātu tiṣhṭhatyakarma-kṛit kāryate hyavaśhaḥ karma sarvaḥ prakṛiti-jair guṇaiḥ

sanskrit: कर्मेन्द्रियाणि संयम्य य आस्ते मनसा स्मरन्। इन्द्रियार्थान्विमूढात्मा मिथ्याचारः स उच्यते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Who sitteth, controlling the organs of action, but dwelling in his mind on the objects of the senses, that bewildered man is called a hypocrite.
transliteration: karmendriyāṇi sanyamya ya āste manasā smaran indriyārthān vimūḍhātmā mithyāchāraḥ sa uchyate

sanskrit: यस्त्विन्द्रियाणि मनसा नियम्यारभतेऽर्जुन। कर्मेन्द्रियैः कर्मयोगमसक्तः स विशिष्यते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: But who, controlling the senses by the mind, O Arjuna, with the organs of action without attachment, performeth yoga by action, he is worthy.
transliteration: yas tvindriyāṇi manasā niyamyārabhate ’rjuna karmendriyaiḥ karma-yogam asaktaḥ sa viśhiṣhyate

sanskrit: नियतं कुरु कर्म त्वं कर्म ज्यायो ह्यकर्मणः। शरीरयात्रापि च ते न प्रसिद्ध्येदकर्मणः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Perform thou right action, for, action is superior to inaction, and, inactive, even the maintenance of thy body would not be possible.
transliteration: niyataṁ kuru karma tvaṁ karma jyāyo hyakarmaṇaḥ śharīra-yātrāpi cha te na prasiddhyed akarmaṇaḥ

sanskrit: यज्ञार्थात्कर्मणोऽन्यत्र लोकोऽयं कर्मबन्धनः। तदर्थं कर्म कौन्तेय मुक्तसंगः समाचर ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: The world is bound by action, unless performed for the sake of sacrifice; for that sake, free from attachment, O son of Kuntî, perform thou action.
transliteration: yajñārthāt karmaṇo ’nyatra loko ’yaṁ karma-bandhanaḥ tad-arthaṁ karma kaunteya mukta-saṅgaḥ samāchara

sanskrit: सहयज्ञाः प्रजाः सृष्ट्वा पुरोवाच प्रजापतिः। अनेन प्रसविष्यध्वमेष वोऽस्त्विष्टकामधुक् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Having in ancient times emanated mankind together with sacrifice, the Lord of emanation said: "By this shall ye propagate; be this to you the giver of desire;
transliteration: saha-yajñāḥ prajāḥ sṛiṣhṭvā purovācha prajāpatiḥ anena prasaviṣhyadhvam eṣha vo ’stviṣhṭa-kāma-dhuk

sanskrit: देवान्भावयतानेन ते देवा भावयन्तु वः। परस्परं भावयन्तः श्रेयः परमवाप्स्यथ ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: "With this nourish ye the Shining Ones, and may the Shining Ones nourish you; thus nourishing one another ye shall reap the supremest good.
transliteration: devān bhāvayatānena te devā bhāvayantu vaḥ parasparaṁ bhāvayantaḥ śhreyaḥ param avāpsyatha

sanskrit: इष्टान्भोगान्हि वो देवा दास्यन्ते यज्ञभाविताः। तैर्दत्तानप्रदायैभ्यो यो भुङ्क्ते स्तेन एव सः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: "For, nourished by sacrifice, the Shining Ones shall bestow on you the enjoyments you desire." A thief verily is he who enjoyeth what is given by Them without returning Them aught.
transliteration: iṣhṭān bhogān hi vo devā dāsyante yajña-bhāvitāḥ tair dattān apradāyaibhyo yo bhuṅkte stena eva saḥ

sanskrit: यज्ञशिष्टाशिनः सन्तो मुच्यन्ते सर्वकिल्बिषैः। भुञ्जते ते त्वघं पापा ये पचन्त्यात्मकारणात् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: The righteous, who eat the remains of the sacrifice, are freed from all sins; but the impious, who dress food for their own sakes, they verily eat sin.
transliteration: yajña-śhiṣhṭāśhinaḥ santo muchyante sarva-kilbiṣhaiḥ bhuñjate te tvaghaṁ pāpā ye pachantyātma-kāraṇāt

sanskrit: अन्नाद्भवन्ति भूतानि पर्जन्यादन्नसम्भवः। यज्ञाद्भवति पर्जन्यो यज्ञः कर्मसमुद्भवः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: From food creatures become; from rain is the production of food; rain proceedeth from sacrifice; sacrifice ariseth out of action.
transliteration: annād bhavanti bhūtāni parjanyād anna-sambhavaḥ yajñād bhavati parjanyo yajñaḥ karma-samudbhavaḥ

sanskrit: कर्म ब्रह्मोद्भवं विद्धि ब्रह्माक्षरसमुद्भवम्। तस्मात्सर्वगतं ब्रह्म नित्यं यज्ञे प्रतिष्ठितम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Know thou that from Brahma action groweth, and Brahma from the Imperishable cometh. Therefore the Eternal , the all-permeating, is ever present in sacrifice.
transliteration: karma brahmodbhavaṁ viddhi brahmākṣhara-samudbhavam tasmāt sarva-gataṁ brahma nityaṁ yajñe pratiṣhṭhitam

sanskrit: एवं प्रवर्तितं चक्रं नानुवर्तयतीह यः। अघायुरिन्द्रियारामो मोघं पार्थ स जीवति ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: He who on earth doth not follow the wheel thus revolving, sinful of life and rejoicing in the senses, he, O son of Pritha, liveth in vain.
transliteration: evaṁ pravartitaṁ chakraṁ nānuvartayatīha yaḥ aghāyur indriyārāmo moghaṁ pārtha sa jīvati

sanskrit: यस्त्वात्मरतिरेव स्यादात्मतृप्तश्च मानवः। आत्मन्येव च सन्तुष्टस्तस्य कार्यं न विद्यते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: But the man who rejoiceth in the Self , with the Self is satisfied, and is content in the Self , for him verily there is nothing to do;
transliteration: yas tvātma-ratir eva syād ātma-tṛiptaśh cha mānavaḥ ātmanyeva cha santuṣhṭas tasya kāryaṁ na vidyate

sanskrit: नैव तस्य कृतेनार्थो नाकृतेनेह कश्चन। न चास्य सर्वभूतेषु कश्िचदर्थव्यपाश्रयः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: For him there is no interest in things done, in this world, nor any in things not done, nor doth any object of his depend on any being.
transliteration: naiva tasya kṛitenārtho nākṛiteneha kaśhchana na chāsya sarva-bhūteṣhu kaśhchid artha-vyapāśhrayaḥ

sanskrit: तस्मादसक्तः सततं कार्यं कर्म समाचर। असक्तो ह्याचरन्कर्म परमाप्नोति पूरुषः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Therefore, without attachment, constantly perform action which is duty, for, by performing action without attachment, man verily reacheth the Supreme.
transliteration: tasmād asaktaḥ satataṁ kāryaṁ karma samāchara asakto hyācharan karma param āpnoti pūruṣhaḥ

sanskrit: कर्मणैव हि संसिद्धिमास्थिता जनकादयः। लोकसंग्रहमेवापि संपश्यन्कर्तुमर्हसि ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Janaka and others indeed attained to perfection by action: then having an eye to the welfare of he world also, thou shouldst perform action.
transliteration: karmaṇaiva hi sansiddhim āsthitā janakādayaḥ loka-saṅgraham evāpi sampaśhyan kartum arhasi

sanskrit: यद्यदाचरति श्रेष्ठस्तत्तदेवेतरो जनः। स यत्प्रमाणं कुरुते लोकस्तदनुवर्तते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Whatsoever a great man doeth, that other men also do; the standard he setteth up, by that the people go.
transliteration: yad yad ācharati śhreṣhṭhas tat tad evetaro janaḥ sa yat pramāṇaṁ kurute lokas tad anuvartate

sanskrit: न मे पार्थास्ति कर्तव्यं त्रिषु लोकेषु किञ्चन। नानवाप्तमवाप्तव्यं वर्त एव च कर्मणि ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: There is nothing in the three worlds, O Pârtha, that should be done by Me, nor anything unattained that might be attained; yet I mingle in action.
transliteration: na me pārthāsti kartavyaṁ triṣhu lokeṣhu kiñchana nānavāptam avāptavyaṁ varta eva cha karmaṇi

sanskrit: यदि ह्यहं न वर्तेयं जातु कर्मण्यतन्द्रितः। मम वर्त्मानुवर्तन्ते मनुष्याः पार्थ सर्वशः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: For if I mingled not ever in action unwearied, men all around would follow My path, O son of Prithâ.
transliteration: yadi hyahaṁ na varteyaṁ jātu karmaṇyatandritaḥ mama vartmānuvartante manuṣhyāḥ pārtha sarvaśhaḥ

sanskrit: उत्सीदेयुरिमे लोका न कुर्यां कर्म चेदहम्। सङ्करस्य च कर्ता स्यामुपहन्यामिमाः प्रजाः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: These worlds would fall into ruin, if I did not perform action; I should be the author of confusion of castes, and should destroy these creatures.
transliteration: utsīdeyur ime lokā na kuryāṁ karma ched aham sankarasya cha kartā syām upahanyām imāḥ prajāḥ

sanskrit: सक्ताः कर्मण्यविद्वांसो यथा कुर्वन्ति भारत। कुर्याद्विद्वांस्तथासक्तश्िचकीर्षुर्लोकसंग्रहम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: As the ignorant act from attachment to action, O Bhârata, so should the wise act without attachment, desiring the welfare of the world.
transliteration: saktāḥ karmaṇyavidvānso yathā kurvanti bhārata kuryād vidvāns tathāsaktaśh chikīrṣhur loka-saṅgraham

sanskrit: न बुद्धिभेदं जनयेदज्ञानां कर्मसङ्गिनाम्। जोषयेत्सर्वकर्माणि विद्वान् युक्तः समाचरन् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Let no wise man unsettle the mind of ignorant people attached to action; but acting in harmony with Me let him render all action attractive.
transliteration: na buddhi-bhedaṁ janayed ajñānāṁ karma-saṅginām joṣhayet sarva-karmāṇi vidvān yuktaḥ samācharan

sanskrit: प्रकृतेः क्रियमाणानि गुणैः कर्माणि सर्वशः। अहङ्कारविमूढात्मा कर्ताऽहमिति मन्यते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: All actions are wrought by the quali ties of nature only. The self, deluded by egoism, thinketh: "I am the doer."
transliteration: prakṛiteḥ kriyamāṇāni guṇaiḥ karmāṇi sarvaśhaḥ ahankāra-vimūḍhātmā kartāham iti manyate

sanskrit: तत्त्ववित्तु महाबाहो गुणकर्मविभागयोः। गुणा गुणेषु वर्तन्त इति मत्वा न सज्जते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: But he, O mighty-armed, who knoweth the essence of the divisions of the qualities and functions, holding that "the qualities move amid the qualities," is not attached.
transliteration: tattva-vit tu mahā-bāho guṇa-karma-vibhāgayoḥ guṇā guṇeṣhu vartanta iti matvā na sajjate

sanskrit: प्रकृतेर्गुणसम्मूढाः सज्जन्ते गुणकर्मसु। तानकृत्स्नविदो मन्दान्कृत्स्नविन्न विचालयेत् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Those deluded by the qualities of nature are attached to the functions of the qualities. The man of perfect knowledge should not unsettle the foolish whose knowledge is imperfect.
transliteration: prakṛiter guṇa-sammūḍhāḥ sajjante guṇa-karmasu tān akṛitsna-vido mandān kṛitsna-vin na vichālayet

sanskrit: मयि सर्वाणि कर्माणि संन्यस्याध्यात्मचेतसा। निराशीर्निर्ममो भूत्वा युध्यस्व विगतज्वरः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Surrendering all actions to Me, with thy thoughts resting on the supreme Self , from hope and egoism freed, and of mental fever cured, engage in battle.
transliteration: mayi sarvāṇi karmāṇi sannyasyādhyātma-chetasā nirāśhīr nirmamo bhūtvā yudhyasva vigata-jvaraḥ

sanskrit: ये मे मतमिदं नित्यमनुतिष्ठन्ति मानवाः। श्रद्धावन्तोऽनसूयन्तो मुच्यन्ते तेऽपि कर्मभिः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Who abide ever in this teaching of Mine full of faith and free from caviling, they too are released from actions.
transliteration: ye me matam idaṁ nityam anutiṣhṭhanti mānavāḥ śhraddhāvanto ’nasūyanto muchyante te ’pi karmabhiḥ

sanskrit: ये त्वेतदभ्यसूयन्तो नानुतिष्ठन्ति मे मतम्। सर्वज्ञानविमूढांस्तान्विद्धि नष्टानचेतसः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Who carp at My teaching and act not thereon, senseless, deluded in all knowledge, know thou these mindless ones as fated to be destroyed.
transliteration: ye tvetad abhyasūyanto nānutiṣhṭhanti me matam sarva-jñāna-vimūḍhāns tān viddhi naṣhṭān achetasaḥ

sanskrit: सदृशं चेष्टते स्वस्याः प्रकृतेर्ज्ञानवानपि। प्रकृतिं यान्ति भूतानि निग्रहः किं करिष्यति ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Even the man of knowledge behaves in conformity with his own nature; beings follow nature; what shall restraint avail?
transliteration: sadṛiśhaṁ cheṣhṭate svasyāḥ prakṛiter jñānavān api prakṛitiṁ yānti bhūtāni nigrahaḥ kiṁ kariṣhyati

sanskrit: इन्द्रियस्येन्द्रियस्यार्थे रागद्वेषौ व्यवस्थितौ। तयोर्न वशमागच्छेत्तौ ह्यस्य परिपन्थिनौ ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Affection and aversion for the objects of sense abide in the senses; let none come under the dominion of these two; they are obstructors of the path.
transliteration: indriyasyendriyasyārthe rāga-dveṣhau vyavasthitau tayor na vaśham āgachchhet tau hyasya paripanthinau

sanskrit: श्रेयान्स्वधर्मो विगुणः परधर्मात्स्वनुष्ठितात्। स्वधर्मे निधनं श्रेयः परधर्मो भयावहः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Better one's own duty, though destitute of merit, than the duty of another, well discharged. Better death in the discharge of one's own duty; the duty of another is full of danger.
transliteration: śhreyān swa-dharmo viguṇaḥ para-dharmāt sv-anuṣhṭhitāt swa-dharme nidhanaṁ śhreyaḥ para-dharmo bhayāvahaḥ

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच अथ केन प्रयुक्तोऽयं पापं चरति पूरुषः। अनिच्छन्नपि वार्ष्णेय बलादिव नियोजितः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Arjuna said: But dragged on by what does a man commit sin, reluctantly indeed, O Varshneya, as it were by force constrained?
transliteration: arjuna uvācha atha kena prayukto ’yaṁ pāpaṁ charati pūruṣhaḥ anichchhann api vārṣhṇeya balād iva niyojitaḥ

sanskrit: श्री भगवानुवाच काम एष क्रोध एष रजोगुणसमुद्भवः। महाशनो महापाप्मा विद्ध्येनमिह वैरिणम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: The Blessed Lord said: It is desire, it is wrath, begotten by the quality of motion; all-consuming, all-polluting, know thou this as our foe here on earth.
transliteration: śhrī bhagavān uvācha kāma eṣha krodha eṣha rajo-guṇa-samudbhavaḥ mahāśhano mahā-pāpmā viddhyenam iha vairiṇam

sanskrit: धूमेनाव्रियते वह्निर्यथाऽऽदर्शो मलेन च। यथोल्बेनावृतो गर्भस्तथा तेनेदमावृतम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: As a flame is enveloped by smoke, as a mirror by dust, as an embryo is wrapped by the amnion, so This is enveloped by it.
transliteration: dhūmenāvriyate vahnir yathādarśho malena cha yatholbenāvṛito garbhas tathā tenedam āvṛitam

sanskrit: आवृतं ज्ञानमेतेन ज्ञानिनो नित्यवैरिणा। कामरूपेण कौन्तेय दुष्पूरेणानलेन च ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Enveloped is wisdom by this constant enemy of the wise in the form of desire, which is insatiable as a flame.
transliteration: āvṛitaṁ jñānam etena jñānino nitya-vairiṇā kāma-rūpeṇa kaunteya duṣhpūreṇānalena cha

sanskrit: इन्द्रियाणि मनो बुद्धिरस्याधिष्ठानमुच्यते। एतैर्विमोहयत्येष ज्ञानमावृत्य देहिनम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: The senses, the mind and the Reason are said to be its seat; by these enveloping wisdom, it bewilders the dweller in the body.
transliteration: indriyāṇi mano buddhir asyādhiṣhṭhānam uchyate etair vimohayatyeṣha jñānam āvṛitya dehinam

sanskrit: तस्मात्त्वमिन्द्रियाण्यादौ नियम्य भरतर्षभ। पाप्मानं प्रजहि ह्येनं ज्ञानविज्ञाननाशनम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Therefore, O best of the Bhâratas, mastering first the senses, do thou slay this thing of sin, destructive of wisdom and knowledge.
transliteration: tasmāt tvam indriyāṇyādau niyamya bharatarṣhabha pāpmānaṁ prajahi hyenaṁ jñāna-vijñāna-nāśhanam

sanskrit: इन्द्रियाणि पराण्याहुरिन्द्रियेभ्यः परं मनः। मनसस्तु परा बुद्धिर्यो बुद्धेः परतस्तु सः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: It is said that the senses are great; greater than the senses is the mind: greater than the mind is the Reason; but what is greater than the Reason, is He.
