transliteration: kleśho ’dhikataras teṣhām avyaktāsakta-chetasām avyaktā hi gatir duḥkhaṁ dehavadbhir avāpyate

sanskrit: ये तु सर्वाणि कर्माणि मयि संन्यस्य मत्पराः।अनन्येनैव योगेन मां ध्यायन्त उपासते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Those verily who, renouncing all actions in Me and intent on Me, worship meditating on Me, with whole-hearted yoga,
transliteration: ye tu sarvāṇi karmāṇi mayi sannyasya mat-paraḥ ananyenaiva yogena māṁ dhyāyanta upāsate

sanskrit: तेषामहं समुद्धर्ता मृत्युसंसारसागरात्।भवामि नचिरात्पार्थ मय्यावेशितचेतसाम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: These I speedily lift up from the ocean of death and existence, O Pârtha, their minds being fixed on Me.
transliteration: teṣhām ahaṁ samuddhartā mṛityu-saṁsāra-sāgarāt bhavāmi na chirāt pārtha mayy āveśhita-chetasām

sanskrit: मय्येव मन आधत्स्व मयि बुद्धिं निवेशय।निवसिष्यसि मय्येव अत ऊर्ध्वं न संशयः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Place thy mind in me, into Me let thy Reason enter; then without doubt thou shalt abide in Me hereafter.
transliteration: mayy eva mana ādhatsva mayi buddhiṁ niveśhaya nivasiṣhyasi mayy eva ata ūrdhvaṁ na sanśhayaḥ

sanskrit: अथ चित्तं समाधातुं न शक्नोषि मयि स्थिरम्।अभ्यासयोगेन ततो मामिच्छाप्तुं धनञ्जय ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: And if thou art not able firmly to fix thy mind on Me, then by the yoga of practice seek to reach Me, O Dhananjaya.
transliteration: atha chittaṁ samādhātuṁ na śhaknoṣhi mayi sthiram abhyāsa-yogena tato mām ichchhāptuṁ dhanañjaya

sanskrit: अभ्यासेऽप्यसमर्थोऽसि मत्कर्मपरमो भव।मदर्थमपि कर्माणि कुर्वन् सिद्धिमवाप्स्यसि ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: If also thou art not equal to constant practice, be intent on My service; performing actions for My sake, thou shalt attain perfection.
transliteration: abhyāse ’py asamartho ’si mat-karma-paramo bhava mad-artham api karmāṇi kurvan siddhim avāpsyasi

sanskrit: अथैतदप्यशक्तोऽसि कर्तुं मद्योगमाश्रितः।सर्वकर्मफलत्यागं ततः कुरु यतात्मवान् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: If even to do this thou hast not strength, then, taking refuge in union with Me, renounce all fruit of action with the self controlled.
transliteration: athaitad apy aśhakto ’si kartuṁ mad-yogam āśhritaḥ sarva-karma-phala-tyāgaṁ tataḥ kuru yatātmavān

sanskrit: श्रेयो हि ज्ञानमभ्यासाज्ज्ञानाद्ध्यानं विशिष्यते।ध्यानात्कर्मफलत्यागस्त्यागाच्छान्तिरनन्तरम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Better indeed is wisdom than constant-practice; than wisdom, meditation is better; than meditation, renunciation of the fruit of action; on renunciation follows peace.
transliteration: śhreyo hi jñānam abhyāsāj jñānād dhyānaṁ viśhiṣhyate dhyānāt karma-phala-tyāgas tyāgāch chhāntir anantaram

sanskrit: अद्वेष्टा सर्वभूतानां मैत्रः करुण एव च।निर्ममो निरहङ्कारः समदुःखसुखः क्षमी ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: He who beareth no ill-will to any being, friendly and compassionate, without attachment and egoism, balanced in pleasure and pain, and forgiving,
transliteration: adveṣhṭā sarva-bhūtānāṁ maitraḥ karuṇa eva cha nirmamo nirahankāraḥ sama-duḥkha-sukhaḥ kṣhamī

sanskrit: सन्तुष्टः सततं योगी यतात्मा दृढनिश्चयः।मय्यर्पितमनोबुद्धिर्यो मद्भक्तः स मे प्रियः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Ever content, harmonious, with the self-controlled, resolute, with mind and Reason dedicated to Me, he, My devotee, is dear to Me.
transliteration: santuṣhṭaḥ satataṁ yogī yatātmā dṛiḍha-niśhchayaḥ mayy arpita-mano-buddhir yo mad-bhaktaḥ sa me priyaḥ

sanskrit: यस्मान्नोद्विजते लोको लोकान्नोद्विजते च यः।हर्षामर्षभयोद्वेगैर्मुक्तो यः स च मे प्रियः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: He from whom the world doth not shrink away, who doth not shrink away from the world, freed from the anxieties of joy, anger, and fear, he is dear to me.
transliteration: yasmān nodvijate loko lokān nodvijate cha yaḥ harṣhāmarṣha-bhayodvegair mukto yaḥ sa cha me priyaḥ

sanskrit: अनपेक्षः शुचिर्दक्ष उदासीनो गतव्यथः।सर्वारम्भपरित्यागी यो मद्भक्तः स मे प्रियः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: He who wants nothing, is pure, expert, passionless, untroubled, renouncing every undertaking, he, My devotee, is dear to Me.
transliteration: anapekṣhaḥ śhuchir dakṣha udāsīno gata-vyathaḥ sarvārambha-parityāgī yo mad-bhaktaḥ sa me priyaḥ

sanskrit: यो न हृष्यति न द्वेष्टि न शोचति न काङ्क्षति।शुभाशुभपरित्यागी भक्ितमान्यः स मे प्रियः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: He who neither loveth nor hateth, nor grieveth, nor desireth, renouncing good and evil, full of devotion, he is dear to Me.
transliteration: yo na hṛiṣhyati na dveṣhṭi na śhochati na kāṅkṣhati śhubhāśhubha-parityāgī bhaktimān yaḥ sa me priyaḥ

sanskrit: समः शत्रौ च मित्रे च तथा मानापमानयोः।शीतोष्णसुखदुःखेषु समः सङ्गविवर्जितः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Alike to foe and friend, and also in fame and ignominy, alike in cold and heat, pleasures and pains, destitute of attachment,
transliteration: samaḥ śhatrau cha mitre cha tathā mānāpamānayoḥ śhītoṣhṇa-sukha-duḥkheṣhu samaḥ saṅga-vivarjitaḥ

sanskrit: तुल्यनिन्दास्तुतिर्मौनी सन्तुष्टो येनकेनचित्।अनिकेतः स्थिरमतिर्भक्ितमान्मे प्रियो नरः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Taking equally praise and reproach, silent, wholly content with what cometh, homeless, firm in mind, full of devotion, that man is dear to Me.
transliteration: tulya-nindā-stutir maunī santuṣhṭo yena kenachit aniketaḥ sthira-matir bhaktimān me priyo naraḥ

sanskrit: ये तु धर्म्यामृतमिदं यथोक्तं पर्युपासते।श्रद्दधाना मत्परमा भक्तास्तेऽतीव मे प्रियाः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: They verily who partake of this life-giving wisdom as taught herein, endued with faith, I their supreme Object, devotees, they are surpassingly dear to Me.
transliteration: ye tu dharmyāmṛitam idaṁ yathoktaṁ paryupāsate śhraddadhānā mat-paramā bhaktās te ’tīva me priyāḥ

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच प्रकृतिं पुरुषं चैव क्षेत्रं क्षेत्रज्ञमेव च। एतद्वेदितुमिच्छामि ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं च केशव ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Arjuna said: Matter and Spirit, even the Field and the Knower of the Field, wisdom and that which ought to be known, these I fain would learn, O Keshava.
transliteration: arjuna uvācha prakṛitiṁ puruṣhaṁ chaiva kṣhetraṁ kṣhetra-jñam eva cha etad veditum ichchhāmi jñānaṁ jñeyaṁ cha keśhava

sanskrit: श्री भगवानुवाचइदं शरीरं कौन्तेय क्षेत्रमित्यभिधीयते।एतद्यो वेत्ति तं प्राहुः क्षेत्रज्ञ इति तद्विदः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: The Blessed Lord said: This body, son of Kuntî, is called the Field; that which knoweth it is called the Knower of the Field by the Sages.
transliteration: śhrī-bhagavān uvācha idaṁ śharīraṁ kaunteya kṣhetram ity abhidhīyate etad yo vetti taṁ prāhuḥ kṣhetra-jña iti tad-vidaḥ

sanskrit: क्षेत्रज्ञं चापि मां विद्धि सर्वक्षेत्रेषु भारत। क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञयोर्ज्ञानं यत्तज्ज्ञानं मतं मम ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Understand Me as the Knower of the Field in all Fields, O Bhârata. Wisdom as to the Field and the Knower of the Field, that in My opinion is the wisdom.
transliteration: kṣhetra-jñaṁ chāpi māṁ viddhi sarva-kṣhetreṣhu bhārata kṣhetra-kṣhetrajñayor jñānaṁ yat taj jñānaṁ mataṁ mama

sanskrit: तत्क्षेत्रं यच्च यादृक् च यद्विकारि यतश्च यत्।स च यो यत्प्रभावश्च तत्समासेन मे श्रृणु ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: What that Field is and of what nature, how modified, and whence it is, and what He is and what His powers, hear that now briefly from Me.
transliteration: tat kṣhetraṁ yach cha yādṛik cha yad-vikāri yataśh cha yat sa cha yo yat-prabhāvaśh cha tat samāsena me śhṛiṇu

sanskrit: ऋषिभिर्बहुधा गीतं छन्दोभिर्विविधैः पृथक्।ब्रह्मसूत्रपदैश्चैव हेतुमद्भिर्विनिश्िचतैः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Rishis have sung in manifold ways, in many various chants, and in decisive Brahma-sûtra verses full of reasonings.
transliteration: ṛiṣhibhir bahudhā gītaṁ chhandobhir vividhaiḥ pṛithak brahma-sūtra-padaiśh chaiva hetumadbhir viniśhchitaiḥ

sanskrit: महाभूतान्यहङ्कारो बुद्धिरव्यक्तमेव च।इन्द्रियाणि दशैकं च पञ्च चेन्द्रियगोचराः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: The great Elements, Individuality, Reason, and also the unmanifested, the ten senses and the one, and the five pastures of the senses;
transliteration: mahā-bhūtāny ahankāro buddhir avyaktam eva cha indriyāṇi daśhaikaṁ cha pañcha chendriya-gocharāḥ

sanskrit: इच्छा द्वेषः सुखं दुःखं सङ्घातश्चेतनाधृतिः।एतत्क्षेत्रं समासेन सविकारमुदाहृतम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Desire, aversion, pleasure, pain, combination, intelligence, firmness; these, briefly described, constitute the Field and its modifications.
transliteration: ichchhā dveṣhaḥ sukhaṁ duḥkhaṁ saṅghātaśh chetanā dhṛitiḥ etat kṣhetraṁ samāsena sa-vikāram udāhṛitam

sanskrit: अमानित्वमदम्भित्वमहिंसा क्षान्तिरार्जवम्।आचार्योपासनं शौचं स्थैर्यमात्मविनिग्रहः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Humility, unpretentiousness, harmlessness, forgiveness, rectitude, service of the teacher, purity, steadfastness, self-control.
transliteration: amānitvam adambhitvam ahinsā kṣhāntir ārjavam āchāryopāsanaṁ śhauchaṁ sthairyam ātma-vinigrahaḥ

sanskrit: इन्द्रियार्थेषु वैराग्यमनहङ्कार एव च।जन्ममृत्युजराव्याधिदुःखदोषानुदर्शनम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Dispassion towards the objects of the senses, and also absence of egoism, insight into the pain and evil of birth, death, old age and sickness,
transliteration: indriyārtheṣhu vairāgyam anahankāra eva cha janma-mṛityu-jarā-vyādhi-duḥkha-doṣhānudarśhanam

sanskrit: असक्ितरनभिष्वङ्गः पुत्रदारगृहादिषु।नित्यं च समचित्तत्वमिष्टानिष्टोपपत्तिषु ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Unattachment, absence of self-identification with son, wife or home, and constant balance of mind in wished-for and unwished-for events,
transliteration: asaktir anabhiṣhvaṅgaḥ putra-dāra-gṛihādiṣhu nityaṁ cha sama-chittatvam iṣhṭāniṣhṭopapattiṣhu

sanskrit: मयि चानन्ययोगेन भक्ितरव्यभिचारिणी।विविक्तदेशसेवित्वमरतिर्जनसंसदि ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Unflinching devotion to Me by yoga, without other objects, resort to sequestered places, absence of enjoyment in the company of men,
transliteration: mayi chānanya-yogena bhaktir avyabhichāriṇī vivikta-deśha-sevitvam aratir jana-sansadi

sanskrit: अध्यात्मज्ञाननित्यत्वं तत्त्वज्ञानार्थदर्शनम्।एतज्ज्ञानमिति प्रोक्तमज्ञानं यदतोन्यथा ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Constancy in the wisdom of the Self, understanding of the object of essential wisdom; that is declared to be the Wisdom; all against it is ignorance.
transliteration: adhyātma-jñāna-nityatvaṁ tattva-jñānārtha-darśhanam etaj jñānam iti proktam ajñānaṁ yad ato ’nyathā

sanskrit: ज्ञेयं यत्तत्प्रवक्ष्यामि यज्ज्ञात्वाऽमृतमश्नुते।अनादिमत्परं ब्रह्म न सत्तन्नासदुच्यते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: I will declare that which ought to be known, that which being known immortality is enjoyed—the beginningless supreme Eternal , called neither being nor non-being.
transliteration: jñeyaṁ yat tat pravakṣhyāmi yaj jñātvāmṛitam aśhnute anādi mat-paraṁ brahma na sat tan nāsad uchyate

sanskrit: सर्वतः पाणिपादं तत्सर्वतोऽक्षिशिरोमुखम्।सर्वतः श्रुतिमल्लोके सर्वमावृत्य तिष्ठति ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Everywhere That hath hands and feet, everywhere eyes, heads, and mouths; all-hearing, He dwelleth in the world, enveloping all,
transliteration: sarvataḥ pāṇi-pādaṁ tat sarvato ’kṣhi-śhiro-mukham sarvataḥ śhrutimal loke sarvam āvṛitya tiṣhṭhati

sanskrit: सर्वेन्द्रियगुणाभासं सर्वेन्द्रियविवर्जितम्।असक्तं सर्वभृच्चैव निर्गुणं गुणभोक्तृ च ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Shining with all sense-faculties without any senses; unattached supporting everything and free from qualities enjoying qualities;
transliteration: sarvendriya-guṇābhāsaṁ sarvendriya-vivarjitam asaktaṁ sarva-bhṛich chaiva nirguṇaṁ guṇa-bhoktṛi cha

sanskrit: बहिरन्तश्च भूतानामचरं चरमेव च।सूक्ष्मत्वात्तदविज्ञेयं दूरस्थं चान्तिके च तत् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Without and within all beings, immovable and also movable; by reason of His subtlety imperceptible; at hand and far away is That .
transliteration: bahir antaśh cha bhūtānām acharaṁ charam eva cha sūkṣhmatvāt tad avijñeyaṁ dūra-sthaṁ chāntike cha tat

sanskrit: अविभक्तं च भूतेषु विभक्तमिव च स्थितम्।भूतभर्तृ च तज्ज्ञेयं ग्रसिष्णु प्रभविष्णु च ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Not divided amid beings, and yet seated distributively; that is to be known as the supporter of beings; He devours and He generates.
transliteration: avibhaktaṁ cha bhūteṣhu vibhaktam iva cha sthitam bhūta-bhartṛi cha taj jñeyaṁ grasiṣhṇu prabhaviṣhṇu cha

sanskrit: ज्योतिषामपि तज्ज्योतिस्तमसः परमुच्यते।ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं ज्ञानगम्यं हृदि सर्वस्य विष्ठितम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: That , the Light of all lights, is said to be beyond darkness; Wisdom, the Object of Wisdom, by Wisdom to be reached, seated in the hearts of all.
transliteration: jyotiṣhām api taj jyotis tamasaḥ param uchyate jñānaṁ jñeyaṁ jñāna-gamyaṁ hṛidi sarvasya viṣhṭhitam

sanskrit: इति क्षेत्रं तथा ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं चोक्तं समासतः।मद्भक्त एतद्विज्ञाय मद्भावायोपपद्यते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Thus the Field, Wisdom and the Object of Wisdom, have been briefly told. My devotee, thus knowing, enters into My Being.
transliteration: iti kṣhetraṁ tathā jñānaṁ jñeyaṁ choktaṁ samāsataḥ mad-bhakta etad vijñāya mad-bhāvāyopapadyate

sanskrit: प्रकृतिं पुरुषं चैव विद्ध्यनादी उभावपि।विकारांश्च गुणांश्चैव विद्धि प्रकृतिसंभवान् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Know thou that Matter and Spirit are both without beginning; and know thou also that modifications and qualities are all Matter-born.
transliteration: prakṛitiṁ puruṣhaṁ chaiva viddhy anādī ubhāv api vikārānśh cha guṇānśh chaiva viddhi prakṛiti-sambhavān

sanskrit: कार्यकारणकर्तृत्वे हेतुः प्रकृतिरुच्यते।पुरुषः सुखदुःखानां भोक्तृत्वे हेतुरुच्यते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Matter is called the cause of the generation of causes and effects; Spirit is called the cause of the enjoyment of pleasure and pain.
transliteration: kārya-kāraṇa-kartṛitve hetuḥ prakṛitir uchyate puruṣhaḥ sukha-duḥkhānāṁ bhoktṛitve hetur uchyate

sanskrit: पुरुषः प्रकृतिस्थो हि भुङ्क्ते प्रकृतिजान्गुणान्।कारणं गुणसङ्गोऽस्य सदसद्योनिजन्मसु ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Spirit seated in Matter useth the qualities born of Matter; attachment to the qualities is the cause of his births in good and evil wombs.
transliteration: puruṣhaḥ prakṛiti-stho hi bhuṅkte prakṛiti-jān guṇān kāraṇaṁ guṇa-saṅgo ’sya sad-asad-yoni-janmasu

sanskrit: उपद्रष्टाऽनुमन्ता च भर्ता भोक्ता महेश्वरः।परमात्मेति चाप्युक्तो देहेऽस्मिन्पुरुषः परः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Supervisor and permitter, supporter, enjoyer, the great Lord, and also the supreme Self ; thus is styled in this body the supreme Spirit.
transliteration: upadraṣhṭānumantā cha bhartā bhoktā maheśhvaraḥ paramātmeti chāpy ukto dehe ’smin puruṣhaḥ paraḥ

sanskrit: य एवं वेत्ति पुरुषं प्रकृतिं च गुणैःसह।सर्वथा वर्तमानोऽपि न स भूयोऽभिजायते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: He who thus knoweth Spirit and Matter with its qualities, in whatsoever condition he may be, he shall not be born again.
transliteration: ya evaṁ vetti puruṣhaṁ prakṛitiṁ cha guṇaiḥ saha sarvathā vartamāno ’pi na sa bhūyo ’bhijāyate

sanskrit: ध्यानेनात्मनि पश्यन्ति केचिदात्मानमात्मना।अन्ये सांख्येन योगेन कर्मयोगेन चापरे ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Some by meditation behold the Self in the self by the Self ; others by the Sânkhya Yoga, and others by the Yoga of Action;
transliteration: dhyānenātmani paśhyanti kechid ātmānam ātmanā anye sānkhyena yogena karma-yogena chāpare

sanskrit: अन्ये त्वेवमजानन्तः श्रुत्वाऽन्येभ्य उपासते।तेऽपि चातितरन्त्येव मृत्युं श्रुतिपरायणाः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Others also, ignorant of this, having heard of it from others, worship; and these also cross beyond death, adhering to what they had heard.
transliteration: anye tv evam ajānantaḥ śhrutvānyebhya upāsate te ’pi chātitaranty eva mṛityuṁ śhruti-parāyaṇāḥ

sanskrit: यावत्सञ्जायते किञ्चित्सत्त्वं स्थावरजङ्गमम्।क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञसंयोगात्तद्विद्धि भरतर्षभ ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Whatsoever creature is born immobile or mobile, know thou, O best of the Bhâratas, that it is from the union between the Field and the Knower of the Field.
transliteration: yāvat sañjāyate kiñchit sattvaṁ sthāvara-jaṅgamam kṣhetra-kṣhetrajña-sanyogāt tad viddhi bharatarṣhabha

sanskrit: समं सर्वेषु भूतेषु तिष्ठन्तं परमेश्वरम्।विनश्यत्स्वविनश्यन्तं यः पश्यति स पश्यति ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Seated equally in all beings, the supreme Lord, unperishing within the perishing—he who thus seeth, he seeth.
transliteration: samaṁ sarveṣhu bhūteṣhu tiṣhṭhantaṁ parameśhvaram vinaśhyatsv avinaśhyantaṁ yaḥ paśhyati sa paśhyati

sanskrit: समं पश्यन्हि सर्वत्र समवस्थितमीश्वरम्।न हिनस्त्यात्मनाऽऽत्मानं ततो याति परां गतिम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Seeing indeed everywhere the same Lord equally dwelling, he doth not destroy the Self , and thus treads the highest Path.
transliteration: samaṁ paśhyan hi sarvatra samavasthitam īśhvaram na hinasty ātmanātmānaṁ tato yāti parāṁ gatim

sanskrit: प्रकृत्यैव च कर्माणि क्रियमाणानि सर्वशः।यः पश्यति तथाऽऽत्मानमकर्तारं स पश्यति ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: He who seeth that Matter verily performeth all actions, and that the Self is actionless, he seeth.
transliteration: prakṛityaiva cha karmāṇi kriyamāṇāni sarvaśhaḥ yaḥ paśhyati tathātmānam akartāraṁ sa paśhyati

sanskrit: यदा भूतपृथग्भावमेकस्थमनुपश्यति।तत एव च विस्तारं ब्रह्म सम्पद्यते तदा ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: When he perceiveth the diversified existence of beings as rooted in One, and spreading forth from it, then he reacheth the Eternal
transliteration: yadā bhūta-pṛithag-bhāvam eka-stham anupaśhyati tata eva cha vistāraṁ brahma sampadyate tadā

sanskrit: अनादित्वान्निर्गुणत्वात्परमात्मायमव्ययः।शरीरस्थोऽपि कौन्तेय न करोति न लिप्यते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Being beginningless and without qualities, the imperishable supreme Self , though seated in the body, O Kaunteya, worketh not nor is affected.
transliteration: anāditvān nirguṇatvāt paramātmāyam avyayaḥ śharīra-stho ’pi kaunteya na karoti na lipyate

sanskrit: यथा सर्वगतं सौक्ष्म्यादाकाशं नोपलिप्यते।सर्वत्रावस्थितो देहे तथाऽऽत्मा नोपलिप्यते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: As the omnipresent ether is not affected, by reason of its subtlety, so seated everywhere in the body the Self is not affected.
transliteration: yathā sarva-gataṁ saukṣhmyād ākāśhaṁ nopalipyate sarvatrāvasthito dehe tathātmā nopalipyate

sanskrit: यथा प्रकाशयत्येकः कृत्स्नं लोकमिमं रविः।क्षेत्रं क्षेत्री तथा कृत्स्नं प्रकाशयति भारत ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: As the one sun illumineth the whole earth, so the Lord of the Field illumineth the whole Field, O Bhârata.
transliteration: yathā prakāśhayaty ekaḥ kṛitsnaṁ lokam imaṁ raviḥ kṣhetraṁ kṣhetrī tathā kṛitsnaṁ prakāśhayati bhārata

sanskrit: क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञयोरेवमन्तरं ज्ञानचक्षुषा।भूतप्रकृतिमोक्षं च ये विदुर्यान्ति ते परम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: They who by the eyes of Wisdom perceive this difference between the Field and the Knower of the Field, and the liberation of beings from Matter they go to the Supreme.
transliteration: kṣhetra-kṣhetrajñayor evam antaraṁ jñāna-chakṣhuṣhā bhūta-prakṛiti-mokṣhaṁ cha ye vidur yānti te param

sanskrit: श्री भगवानुवाचपरं भूयः प्रवक्ष्यामि ज्ञानानां ज्ञानमुत्तमम्।यज्ज्ञात्वा मुनयः सर्वे परां सिद्धिमितो गताः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: The Blessed Lord said: I will again proclaim that supreme Wisdom, of all wisdom the best, which having known, all the Sages have gone hence to the supreme Perfection.
transliteration: śhrī-bhagavān uvācha paraṁ bhūyaḥ pravakṣhyāmi jñānānāṁ jñānam uttamam yaj jñātvā munayaḥ sarve parāṁ siddhim ito gatāḥ

sanskrit: इदं ज्ञानमुपाश्रित्य मम साधर्म्यमागताः।सर्गेऽपि नोपजायन्ते प्रलये न व्यथन्ति च ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Having taken refuge in this Wisdom and being assimilated to My own nature, they are not re-born even in the emanation of a universe, nor are disquieted in the dissolution.
transliteration: idaṁ jñānam upāśhritya mama sādharmyam āgatāḥ sarge ’pi nopajāyante pralaye na vyathanti cha

sanskrit: मम योनिर्महद्ब्रह्म तस्मिन् गर्भं दधाम्यहम्।संभवः सर्वभूतानां ततो भवति भारत ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: My womb is the great Eternal ; in that I place the germ; thence cometh the birth of all beings, O Bhârata.
transliteration: mama yonir mahad brahma tasmin garbhaṁ dadhāmy aham sambhavaḥ sarva-bhūtānāṁ tato bhavati bhārata

sanskrit: सर्वयोनिषु कौन्तेय मूर्तयः सम्भवन्ति याः।तासां ब्रह्म महद्योनिरहं बीजप्रदः पिता ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: In whatsoever wombs mortals are produced, O Kaunteya, the great Eternal is their womb, I their generating father.
transliteration: sarva-yoniṣhu kaunteya mūrtayaḥ sambhavanti yāḥ tāsāṁ brahma mahad yonir ahaṁ bīja-pradaḥ pitā

sanskrit: सत्त्वं रजस्तम इति गुणाः प्रकृतिसंभवाः।निबध्नन्ति महाबाहो देहे देहिनमव्ययम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Harmony, Motion, Inertia, such are the qualities, Matter-born; they bind fast in the body, O great-armed one, the indestructible dweller in the body.
transliteration: sattvaṁ rajas tama iti guṇāḥ prakṛiti-sambhavāḥ nibadhnanti mahā-bāho dehe dehinam avyayam

sanskrit: तत्र सत्त्वं निर्मलत्वात्प्रकाशकमनामयम्।सुखसङ्गेन बध्नाति ज्ञानसङ्गेन चानघ ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Of these Harmony from its stainlessness, luminous and healthy, bindeth by the attachment to bliss and the attachment to wisdom, O sinless one.
transliteration: tatra sattvaṁ nirmalatvāt prakāśhakam anāmayam sukha-saṅgena badhnāti jñāna-saṅgena chānagha

sanskrit: रजो रागात्मकं विद्धि तृष्णासङ्गसमुद्भवम्।तन्निबध्नाति कौन्तेय कर्मसङ्गेन देहिनम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Motion, the passion nature, know thou, is the source of attachment and thirst for life, O Kaunteya, that bindeth the dweller in the body by the attachment to action.
transliteration: rajo rāgātmakaṁ viddhi tṛiṣhṇā-saṅga-samudbhavam tan nibadhnāti kaunteya karma-saṅgena dehinam

sanskrit: तमस्त्वज्ञानजं विद्धि मोहनं सर्वदेहिनाम्।प्रमादालस्यनिद्राभिस्तन्निबध्नाति भारत ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: But Inertia, know thou, born of unwisdom, is the deluder of all dwellers in the body; that bindeth by heedlessness, indolence and sloth, O Bharata .
transliteration: tamas tv ajñāna-jaṁ viddhi mohanaṁ sarva-dehinām pramādālasya-nidrābhis tan nibadhnāti bhārata

sanskrit: सत्त्वं सुखे सञ्जयति रजः कर्मणि भारत।ज्ञानमावृत्य तु तमः प्रमादे सञ्जयत्युत ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Harmony attacheth to bliss, Motion to action O Bharata . Inertia, verily, having shrouded wisdom, attacheth on the contrary to heedlessness.
transliteration: sattvaṁ sukhe sañjayati rajaḥ karmaṇi bhārata jñānam āvṛitya tu tamaḥ pramāde sañjayaty uta

sanskrit: रजस्तमश्चाभिभूय सत्त्वं भवति भारत।रजः सत्त्वं तमश्चैव तमः सत्त्वं रजस्तथा ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Now Harmony prevaileth, having overpowered Motion and Inertia, O Bhârata; now Motion, having overpowered Harmony and Inertia; and now Inertia, having overpowered Harmony and Motion.
transliteration: rajas tamaśh chābhibhūya sattvaṁ bhavati bhārata rajaḥ sattvaṁ tamaśh chaiva tamaḥ sattvaṁ rajas tathā

sanskrit: सर्वद्वारेषु देहेऽस्मिन्प्रकाश उपजायते।ज्ञानं यदा तदा विद्याद्विवृद्धं सत्त्वमित्युत ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: When the wisdom-light streameth forth from all the gates of the body, then it may be known that Harmony is increasing.
transliteration: sarva-dvāreṣhu dehe ’smin prakāśha upajāyate jñānaṁ yadā tadā vidyād vivṛiddhaṁ sattvam ity uta

sanskrit: लोभः प्रवृत्तिरारम्भः कर्मणामशमः स्पृहा।रजस्येतानि जायन्ते विवृद्धे भरतर्षभ ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Greed, outgoing energy, undertaking of actions, restlessness, desire—these are born of the increase of Motion, O best of the Bhâratas.
transliteration: lobhaḥ pravṛittir ārambhaḥ karmaṇām aśhamaḥ spṛihā rajasy etāni jāyante vivṛiddhe bharatarṣhabha

sanskrit: अप्रकाशोऽप्रवृत्तिश्च प्रमादो मोह एव च।तमस्येतानि जायन्ते विवृद्धे कुरुनन्दन ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Darkness, stagnation and heedlessness and also delusion—these are born of the increase of Inertia, O joy of the Kurus.
transliteration: aprakāśho ’pravṛittiśh cha pramādo moha eva cha tamasy etāni jāyante vivṛiddhe kuru-nandana

sanskrit: यदा सत्त्वे प्रवृद्धे तु प्रलयं याति देहभृत्।तदोत्तमविदां लोकानमलान्प्रतिपद्यते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: If Harmony verily prevaileth when the embodied goeth to dissolution, then he goeth forth to the spotless worlds of the great Sages.
transliteration: yadā sattve pravṛiddhe tu pralayaṁ yāti deha-bhṛit tadottama-vidāṁ lokān amalān pratipadyate

sanskrit: रजसि प्रलयं गत्वा कर्मसङ्गिषु जायते।तथा प्रलीनस्तमसि मूढयोनिषु जायते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Having gone to dissolution in Motion, he is born among those attached to action; if dissolved in Inertia, he is born in the wombs of the senseless.
transliteration: rajasi pralayaṁ gatvā karma-saṅgiṣhu jāyate tathā pralīnas tamasi mūḍha-yoniṣhu jāyate

sanskrit: कर्मणः सुकृतस्याहुः सात्त्विकं निर्मलं फलम्।रजसस्तु फलं दुःखमज्ञानं तमसः फलम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: It is said the fruit of a good action is harmonious and spotless; verily the fruit of Motion is pain, and the fruit of Inertia unwisdom
transliteration: karmaṇaḥ sukṛitasyāhuḥ sāttvikaṁ nirmalaṁ phalam rajasas tu phalaṁ duḥkham ajñānaṁ tamasaḥ phalam

sanskrit: सत्त्वात्सञ्जायते ज्ञानं रजसो लोभ एव च।प्रमादमोहौ तमसो भवतोऽज्ञानमेव च ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: From Harmony wisdom is born, and also greed from Motion; heedlessness and delusion are of Inertia and also unwisdom.
transliteration: sattvāt sañjāyate jñānaṁ rajaso lobha eva cha pramāda-mohau tamaso bhavato ’jñānam eva cha

sanskrit: ऊर्ध्वं गच्छन्ति सत्त्वस्था मध्ये तिष्ठन्ति राजसाः।जघन्यगुणवृत्तिस्था अधो गच्छन्ति तामसाः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: They rise upwards who are settled in Harmony; the Active dwell in the mid-most place: the Inert go downwards, enveloped in the vilest
transliteration: ūrdhvaṁ gachchhanti sattva-sthā madhye tiṣhṭhanti rājasāḥ jaghanya-guṇa-vṛitti-sthā adho gachchhanti tāmasāḥ

sanskrit: नान्यं गुणेभ्यः कर्तारं यदा द्रष्टानुपश्यति।गुणेभ्यश्च परं वेत्ति मद्भावं सोऽधिगच्छति ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: When the Seer perceiveth no agent other than the qualities, and knoweth That which is higher than the qualities, he entereth into My Nature.
transliteration: nānyaṁ guṇebhyaḥ kartāraṁ yadā draṣhṭānupaśhyati guṇebhyaśh cha paraṁ vetti mad-bhāvaṁ so ’dhigachchhati

sanskrit: गुणानेतानतीत्य त्रीन्देही देहसमुद्भवान्।जन्ममृत्युजरादुःखैर्विमुक्तोऽमृतमश्नुते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: When the dweller in the body hath crossed over these three qualities, whence all bodies have been produced, liberated from birth, death, old age and sorrow, he drinketh the nectar of immortality.
transliteration: guṇān etān atītya trīn dehī deha-samudbhavān janma-mṛityu-jarā-duḥkhair vimukto ’mṛitam aśhnute

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाचकैर्लिंगैस्त्रीन्गुणानेतानतीतो भवति प्रभो।किमाचारः कथं चैतांस्त्रीन्गुणानतिवर्तते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Arjuna said: What are the marks of him who hath crossed over the three qualities, O Lord? How acteth he, and how doth he go beyond these three qualities?
transliteration: arjuna uvācha kair liṅgais trīn guṇān etān atīto bhavati prabho kim āchāraḥ kathaṁ chaitāns trīn guṇān ativartate

sanskrit: श्री भगवानुवाचप्रकाशं च प्रवृत्तिं च मोहमेव च पाण्डव।न द्वेष्टि सम्प्रवृत्तानि न निवृत्तानि काङ्क्षति ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: The Blessed Lord said: He, O Pândava, who hateth not radiance, nor outgoing energy, nor even delusion, when present, nor longeth after them, absent;
transliteration: śhrī-bhagavān uvācha prakāśhaṁ cha pravṛittiṁ cha moham eva cha pāṇḍava na dveṣhṭi sampravṛittāni na nivṛittāni kāṅkṣhati

sanskrit: उदासीनवदासीनो गुणैर्यो न विचाल्यते।गुणा वर्तन्त इत्येव योऽवतिष्ठति नेङ्गते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: He who, seated as a neutral, is unshaken by the qualities; who, saying, "The qualities revolve," standeth apart immovable.
transliteration: udāsīna-vad āsīno guṇair yo na vichālyate guṇā vartanta ity evaṁ yo ’vatiṣhṭhati neṅgate

sanskrit: समदुःखसुखः स्वस्थः समलोष्टाश्मकाञ्चनः।तुल्यप्रियाप्रियो धीरस्तुल्यनिन्दात्मसंस्तुतिः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Balanced in pleasure and pain, self-reliant, to whom a lump of earth, a rock and gold are alike, the same to loved and unloved, firm, the same in censure and in praise,
transliteration: sama-duḥkha-sukhaḥ sva-sthaḥ sama-loṣhṭāśhma-kāñchanaḥ tulya-priyāpriyo dhīras tulya-nindātma-sanstutiḥ

sanskrit: मानापमानयोस्तुल्यस्तुल्यो मित्रारिपक्षयोः।सर्वारम्भपरित्यागी गुणातीतः स उच्यते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: The same in honour and ignominy, the same to friend and foe, abandoning all undertakings—he is said to have crossed over the qualities.
transliteration: mānāpamānayos tulyas tulyo mitrāri-pakṣhayoḥ sarvārambha-parityāgī guṇātītaḥ sa uchyate

sanskrit: मां च योऽव्यभिचारेण भक्ितयोगेन सेवते।स गुणान्समतीत्यैतान् ब्रह्मभूयाय कल्पते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: And he who serveth Me exclusively by the Yoga of devotion, he, crossing beyond the qualities, he is fit to become the Eternal .
transliteration: māṁ cha yo ’vyabhichāreṇa bhakti-yogena sevate sa guṇān samatītyaitān brahma-bhūyāya kalpate

sanskrit: ब्रह्मणो हि प्रतिष्ठाऽहममृतस्याव्ययस्य च।शाश्वतस्य च धर्मस्य सुखस्यैकान्तिकस्य च ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: For I am the abode of the Eternal , and of the indestructible nectar of immortality, of immemorial righteousness, and of unending bliss.
transliteration: brahmaṇo hi pratiṣhṭhāham amṛitasyāvyayasya cha śhāśhvatasya cha dharmasya sukhasyaikāntikasya cha

sanskrit: श्री भगवानुवाचऊर्ध्वमूलमधःशाखमश्वत्थं प्राहुरव्ययम्।छन्दांसि यस्य पर्णानि यस्तं वेद स वेदवित् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: The Blessed Lord said: With roots above, branches below, the Asvattha is said to be indestructible; the leaves of it are hymns; he who knoweth it is a Veda-knower.
transliteration: śhrī-bhagavān uvācha ūrdhva-mūlam adhaḥ-śhākham aśhvatthaṁ prāhur avyayam chhandānsi yasya parṇāni yas taṁ veda sa veda-vit

sanskrit: अधश्चोर्ध्वं प्रसृतास्तस्य शाखा गुणप्रवृद्धा विषयप्रवालाः।अधश्च मूलान्यनुसन्ततानि कर्मानुबन्धीनि मनुष्यलोके ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Downwards and upwards spread the branches of it, nourished by the qualities; the objects of the senses its buds; and its roots grow downwards, the bonds of action in the world of men.
transliteration: adhaśh chordhvaṁ prasṛitās tasya śhākhā guṇa-pravṛiddhā viṣhaya-pravālāḥ adhaśh cha mūlāny anusantatāni karmānubandhīni manuṣhya-loke

sanskrit: न रूपमस्येह तथोपलभ्यते नान्तो न चादिर्न च संप्रतिष्ठा।अश्वत्थमेनं सुविरूढमूल मसङ्गशस्त्रेण दृढेन छित्त्वा ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Nor here may be acquired knowledge of its form, nor its end, nor its origin, nor its rooting-place; this strongly rooted Asvattha having been cut down by the unswerving weapon of non-attachment,
transliteration: na rūpam asyeha tathopalabhyate nānto na chādir na cha sampratiṣhṭhā aśhvattham enaṁ su-virūḍha-mūlam asaṅga-śhastreṇa dṛiḍhena chhittvā

sanskrit: ततः पदं तत्परिमार्गितव्य यस्मिन्गता न निवर्तन्ति भूयः।तमेव चाद्यं पुरुषं प्रपद्ये यतः प्रवृत्तिः प्रसृता पुराणी ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: That path beyond may be sought, treading which there is no return. I go indeed to that Primal Man, whence the ancient energy forth-streamed.
transliteration: tataḥ padaṁ tat parimārgitavyaṁ yasmin gatā na nivartanti bhūyaḥ tam eva chādyaṁ puruṣhaṁ prapadye yataḥ pravṛittiḥ prasṛitā purāṇī

sanskrit: निर्मानमोहा जितसङ्गदोषा अध्यात्मनित्या विनिवृत्तकामाः।द्वन्द्वैर्विमुक्ताः सुखदुःखसंज्ञै र्गच्छन्त्यमूढाः पदमव्ययं तत् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Without pride and delusion, victorious over the vice of attachment, dwelling constantly in the Self , desire pacified, liberated from the pairs of opposites known as pleasure and pain, they tread, undeluded, that indestructible path.
transliteration: nirmāna-mohā jita-saṅga-doṣhā adhyātma-nityā vinivṛitta-kāmāḥ dvandvair vimuktāḥ sukha-duḥkha-sanjñair gachchhanty amūḍhāḥ padam avyayaṁ tat

sanskrit: न तद्भासयते सूर्यो न शशाङ्को न पावकः।यद्गत्वा न निवर्तन्ते तद्धाम परमं मम ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Nor doth the sun lighten there, nor moon, nor fire; having gone thither they return not; that is My supreme abode.
transliteration: na tad bhāsayate sūryo na śhaśhāṅko na pāvakaḥ yad gatvā na nivartante tad dhāma paramaṁ mama

sanskrit: ममैवांशो जीवलोके जीवभूतः सनातनः।मनःषष्ठानीन्द्रियाणि प्रकृतिस्थानि कर्षति ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: A portion of Mine own Self, transformed in the world of life into an immortal Spirit, draweth round itself the senses of which the mind is the sixth, veiled in matter.
transliteration: mamaivānśho jīva-loke jīva-bhūtaḥ sanātanaḥ manaḥ-ṣhaṣhṭhānīndriyāṇi prakṛiti-sthāni karṣhati

sanskrit: शरीरं यदवाप्नोति यच्चाप्युत्क्रामतीश्वरः।गृहीत्वैतानि संयाति वायुर्गन्धानिवाशयात् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: When the Lord acquireth a body and when He abandoneth it, He seizeth these and goeth with them, as the wind takes fragrances from their retreats
transliteration: śharīraṁ yad avāpnoti yach chāpy utkrāmatīśhvaraḥ gṛihītvaitāni sanyāti vāyur gandhān ivāśhayāt

sanskrit: श्रोत्रं चक्षुः स्पर्शनं च रसनं घ्राणमेव च।अधिष्ठाय मनश्चायं विषयानुपसेवते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Enshrined in the ear, the eye, the touch, the taste and the smell, and in the mind also, He enjoyeth the objects of the senses
transliteration: śhrotraṁ chakṣhuḥ sparśhanaṁ cha rasanaṁ ghrāṇam eva cha adhiṣhṭhāya manaśh chāyaṁ viṣhayān upasevate

sanskrit: उत्क्रामन्तं स्थितं वापि भुञ्जानं वा गुणान्वितम्।विमूढा नानुपश्यन्ति पश्यन्ति ज्ञानचक्षुषः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: The deluded do not perceive (Him) when He departeth or stayeth or enjoyeth, swayed by the qualities; the wisdom-eyed perceive.
transliteration: utkrāmantaṁ sthitaṁ vāpi bhuñjānaṁ vā guṇānvitam vimūḍhā nānupaśhyanti paśhyanti jñāna-chakṣhuṣhaḥ

sanskrit: यतन्तो योगिनश्चैनं पश्यन्त्यात्मन्यवस्थितम्।यतन्तोऽप्यकृतात्मानो नैनं पश्यन्त्यचेतसः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Yogîs also, struggling, perceive Him, established in the Self ; but though struggling, the unintelligent perceive Him not, their selves untrained.
transliteration: yatanto yoginaśh chainaṁ paśhyanty ātmany avasthitam yatanto ‘py akṛitātmāno nainaṁ paśhyanty achetasaḥ

sanskrit: यदादित्यगतं तेजो जगद्भासयतेऽखिलम्।यच्चन्द्रमसि यच्चाग्नौ तत्तेजो विद्धि मामकम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: That splendour issuing from the sun that enlighteneth the whole world, that which is in the moon and in fire, that splendour know as from Me.
transliteration: yad āditya-gataṁ tejo jagad bhāsayate ’khilam yach chandramasi yach chāgnau tat tejo viddhi māmakam

sanskrit: गामाविश्य च भूतानि धारयाम्यहमोजसा।पुष्णामि चौषधीः सर्वाः सोमो भूत्वा रसात्मकः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Permeating the soil, I support beings by my vital energy, and having become the delicious Soma I nourish all plants.
transliteration: gām āviśhya cha bhūtāni dhārayāmy aham ojasā puṣhṇāmi chauṣhadhīḥ sarvāḥ somo bhūtvā rasātmakaḥ

sanskrit: अहं वैश्वानरो भूत्वा प्राणिनां देहमाश्रितः।प्राणापानसमायुक्तः पचाम्यन्नं चतुर्विधम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: I, having become the Fire of Life, take possession of the bodies of breathing things, and united with the life-breaths, I digest the four kinds of food.
transliteration: ahaṁ vaiśhvānaro bhūtvā prāṇināṁ deham āśhritaḥ prāṇāpāna-samāyuktaḥ pachāmy annaṁ chatur-vidham

sanskrit: सर्वस्य चाहं हृदि सन्निविष्टो मत्तः स्मृतिर्ज्ञानमपोहनं च।वेदैश्च सर्वैरहमेव वेद्यो वेदान्तकृद्वेदविदेव चाहम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: And I am seated in the hearts of all, and from Me memory and wisdom and their absence. And that which is to be known in all the Vedas am I; and I indeed the Veda-knower and the author of the Vedanta.
transliteration: sarvasya chāhaṁ hṛidi sanniviṣhṭo mattaḥ smṛitir jñānam apohanaṁ cha vedaiśh cha sarvair aham eva vedyo vedānta-kṛid veda-vid eva chāham

sanskrit: द्वाविमौ पुरुषौ लोके क्षरश्चाक्षर एव च।क्षरः सर्वाणि भूतानि कूटस्थोऽक्षर उच्यते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: There are two Energies in this world, the destructible and the indestructible; the destructible is all beings, the unchanging is called the indestructible.
transliteration: dvāv imau puruṣhau loke kṣharaśh chākṣhara eva cha kṣharaḥ sarvāṇi bhūtāni kūṭa-stho ’kṣhara uchyate

sanskrit: उत्तमः पुरुषस्त्वन्यः परमात्मेत्युदाहृतः।यो लोकत्रयमाविश्य बिभर्त्यव्यय ईश्वरः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: The highest Energy is verily Another, declared as the Supreme Self , He who, pervading all, sustaineth the three worlds, the indestructible Lord.
transliteration: uttamaḥ puruṣhas tv anyaḥ paramātmety udāhṛitaḥ yo loka-trayam āviśhya bibharty avyaya īśhvaraḥ

sanskrit: यस्मात्क्षरमतीतोऽहमक्षरादपि चोत्तमः।अतोऽस्मि लोके वेदे च प्रथितः पुरुषोत्तमः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Since I excel the destructible, and am more excellent also than the indestructible, in the world and in the Veda I am proclaimed the Supreme Spirit.
transliteration: yasmāt kṣharam atīto ’ham akṣharād api chottamaḥ ato ’smi loke vede cha prathitaḥ puruṣhottamaḥ

sanskrit: यो मामेवमसम्मूढो जानाति पुरुषोत्तमम्।स सर्वविद्भजति मां सर्वभावेन भारत ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: He who undeluded knoweth Me thus as the Supreme Spirit, he, all-knowing, worshippeth Me with his whole being, O Bhârata.
transliteration: yo mām evam asammūḍho jānāti puruṣhottamam sa sarva-vid bhajati māṁ sarva-bhāvena bhārata

sanskrit: इति गुह्यतमं शास्त्रमिदमुक्तं मयाऽनघ।एतद्बुद्ध्वा बुद्धिमान्स्यात्कृतकृत्यश्च भारत ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Thus by Me this most secret teaching hath been told O sinless one. This known, he hath become illuminated and hath finished his work, O Bhârata.
transliteration: iti guhyatamaṁ śhāstram idam uktaṁ mayānagha etad buddhvā buddhimān syāt kṛita-kṛityaśh cha bhārata

sanskrit: श्री भगवानुवाच अभयं सत्त्वसंशुद्धिः ज्ञानयोगव्यवस्थितिः। दानं दमश्च यज्ञश्च स्वाध्यायस्तप आर्जवम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: The Blessed Lord said: Fearlessness, cleanness of life, steadfastness in the Yoga of wisdom, almsgiving, self-restraint and sacrifice and study of the Scriptures, austerity and straightforwardness,
transliteration: śhrī-bhagavān uvācha abhayaṁ sattva-sanśhuddhir jñāna-yoga-vyavasthitiḥ dānaṁ damaśh cha yajñaśh cha svādhyāyas tapa ārjavam

sanskrit: अहिंसा सत्यमक्रोधस्त्यागः शान्तिरपैशुनम्।दया भूतेष्वलोलुप्त्वं मार्दवं ह्रीरचापलम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Harmlessness, truth, absence of wrath, renunciation, peacefulness, absence of crookedness, compassion to living beings, uncovetousness, mildness, modesty, absence of fickleness,
transliteration: ahinsā satyam akrodhas tyāgaḥ śhāntir apaiśhunam dayā bhūteṣhv aloluptvaṁ mārdavaṁ hrīr achāpalam

sanskrit: तेजः क्षमा धृतिः शौचमद्रोहो नातिमानिता। भवन्ति सम्पदं दैवीमभिजातस्य भारत ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Vigour, forgiveness, fortitude, purity, absence of envy and pride—these are his who is born with the divine properties, O Bhârata.
transliteration: tejaḥ kṣhamā dhṛitiḥ śhaucham adroho nāti-mānitā bhavanti sampadaṁ daivīm abhijātasya bhārata

sanskrit: दम्भो दर्पोऽभिमानश्च क्रोधः पारुष्यमेव च।अज्ञानं चाभिजातस्य पार्थ सम्पदमासुरीम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Hypocrisy, arrogance and conceit, wrath and also harshness and unwisdom are his who is born, O Pârtha, with demoniacal properties.
transliteration: dambho darpo ’bhimānaśh cha krodhaḥ pāruṣhyam eva cha ajñānaṁ chābhijātasya pārtha sampadam āsurīm

sanskrit: दैवी सम्पद्विमोक्षाय निबन्धायासुरी मता।मा शुचः सम्पदं दैवीमभिजातोऽसि पाण्डव ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: The divine properties are deemed to be for liberation, the demoniacal for bondage. Grieve not, thou art born with divine properties, O Pândava.
transliteration: daivī sampad vimokṣhāya nibandhāyāsurī matā mā śhuchaḥ sampadaṁ daivīm abhijāto ’si pāṇḍava

sanskrit: द्वौ भूतसर्गौ लोकेऽस्मिन् दैव आसुर एव च।दैवो विस्तरशः प्रोक्त आसुरं पार्थ मे श्रृणु ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Twofold is the animal creation in this world, the divine and the demoniacal: the divine hath been described at length: hear from Me, O Pârtha, the demoniacal.
transliteration: dvau bhūta-sargau loke ’smin daiva āsura eva cha daivo vistaraśhaḥ prokta āsuraṁ pārtha me śhṛiṇu

sanskrit: प्रवृत्तिं च निवृत्तिं च जना न विदुरासुराः।न शौचं नापि चाचारो न सत्यं तेषु विद्यते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Demoniacal men know neither right energy nor right abstinence; nor purity, nor even propriety, nor truth is in them.
transliteration: pravṛittiṁ cha nivṛittiṁ cha janā na vidur āsurāḥ na śhauchaṁ nāpi chāchāro na satyaṁ teṣhu vidyate

sanskrit: असत्यमप्रतिष्ठं ते जगदाहुरनीश्वरम्।अपरस्परसम्भूतं किमन्यत्कामहैतुकम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: "The universe is without truth, without basis," they say, "without a God; brought about by mutual union, and caused by lust and nothing else."
transliteration: asatyam apratiṣhṭhaṁ te jagad āhur anīśhvaram aparaspara-sambhūtaṁ kim anyat kāma-haitukam

sanskrit: एतां दृष्टिमवष्टभ्य नष्टात्मानोऽल्पबुद्धयः।प्रभवन्त्युग्रकर्माणः क्षयाय जगतोऽहिताः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Holding this view, these ruined selves of small understanding, of fierce deeds, come forth as enemies for the destruction of the world.
transliteration: etāṁ dṛiṣhṭim avaṣhṭabhya naṣhṭātmāno ’lpa-buddhayaḥ prabhavanty ugra-karmāṇaḥ kṣhayāya jagato ’hitāḥ

sanskrit: काममाश्रित्य दुष्पूरं दम्भमानमदान्विताः।मोहाद्गृहीत्वासद्ग्राहान्प्रवर्तन्तेऽशुचिव्रताः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Surrendering themselves to insatiable desires, possessed with vanity, conceit and arrogance, holding evil ideas through delusion, they engage in action with impure resolves.
transliteration: kāmam āśhritya duṣhpūraṁ dambha-māna-madānvitāḥ mohād gṛihītvāsad-grāhān pravartante ’śhuchi-vratāḥ

sanskrit: चिन्तामपरिमेयां च प्रलयान्तामुपाश्रिताः।कामोपभोगपरमा एतावदिति निश्िचताः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Giving themselves over to unmeasured thought whose end is death, regarding the gratification of desires as the highest, feeling sure that this is all,
transliteration: chintām aparimeyāṁ cha pralayāntām upāśhritāḥ kāmopabhoga-paramā etāvad iti niśhchitāḥ

sanskrit: आशापाशशतैर्बद्धाः कामक्रोधपरायणाः।ईहन्ते कामभोगार्थमन्यायेनार्थसञ्चयान् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Held in bondage by a hundred ties of expectation, given over to lust and anger, they strive to obtain by unlawful means hoards of wealth for sensual enjoyments.
transliteration: āśhā-pāśha-śhatair baddhāḥ kāma-krodha-parāyaṇāḥ īhante kāma-bhogārtham anyāyenārtha-sañchayān

sanskrit: इदमद्य मया लब्धमिमं प्राप्स्ये मनोरथम्।इदमस्तीदमपि मे भविष्यति पुनर्धनम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: "This to-day by me hath been won, that purpose I shall gain; this wealth is mine already, and also this shall be mine in future.
transliteration: idam adya mayā labdham imaṁ prāpsye manoratham idam astīdam api me bhaviṣhyati punar dhanam

sanskrit: असौ मया हतः शत्रुर्हनिष्ये चापरानपि।ईश्वरोऽहमहं भोगी सिद्धोऽहं बलवान्सुखी ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: "I have slain this enemy, and others also I shall slay. I am the Lord, I am the enjoyer, I am perfect, powerful, happy;
transliteration: asau mayā hataḥ śhatrur haniṣhye chāparān api īśhvaro ’ham ahaṁ bhogī siddho ’haṁ balavān sukhī

sanskrit: आढ्योऽभिजनवानस्मि कोऽन्योऽस्ति सदृशो मया।यक्ष्ये दास्यामि मोदिष्य इत्यज्ञानविमोहिताः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: "I am wealthy, well-born; what other is there that is like unto me? I will sacrifice, I will give alms, I will rejoice." Thus deluded by unwisdom,
transliteration: āḍhyo ’bhijanavān asmi ko ’nyo ’sti sadṛiśho mayā yakṣhye dāsyāmi modiṣhya ity ajñāna-vimohitāḥ aneka-chitta-vibhrāntā moha-jāla-samāvṛitāḥ prasaktāḥ kāma-bhogeṣhu patanti narake ’śhuchau

sanskrit: अनेकचित्तविभ्रान्ता मोहजालसमावृताः।प्रसक्ताः कामभोगेषु पतन्ति नरकेऽशुचौ ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Bewildered by numerous thoughts, enmeshed in the web of delusion, addicted to the gratification of desire, they fall downwards into a foul hell.
transliteration: aneka-citta-vibhrāntā moha-jāla-samāvṛtāḥ prasaktāḥ kāma-bhogeṣu patanti narake 'śucau

sanskrit: आत्मसम्भाविताः स्तब्धा धनमानमदान्विताः।यजन्ते नामयज्ञैस्ते दम्भेनाविधिपूर्वकम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Self-glorifying, stubborn, filled with the pride and intoxication of wealth, they perform lip-sacrifices for ostentation, contrary to scriptural ordinance.
transliteration: ātma-sambhāvitāḥ stabdhā dhana-māna-madānvitāḥ yajante nāma-yajñais te dambhenāvidhi-pūrvakam

sanskrit: अहङ्कारं बलं दर्पं कामं क्रोधं च संश्रिताः।मामात्मपरदेहेषु प्रद्विषन्तोऽभ्यसूयकाः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Given over to egoism, power, insolence, lust and wrath, these malicious ones hate Me in the bodies of others and in their own.
transliteration: ahankāraṁ balaṁ darpaṁ kāmaṁ krodhaṁ cha sanśhritāḥ mām ātma-para-deheṣhu pradviṣhanto ’bhyasūyakāḥ

sanskrit: तानहं द्विषतः क्रूरान्संसारेषु नराधमान्।क्षिपाम्यजस्रमशुभानासुरीष्वेव योनिषु ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: These haters, evil, pitiless, vilest among men in the world, I ever throw down into demoniacal wombs.
transliteration: tān ahaṁ dviṣhataḥ krūrān sansāreṣhu narādhamān kṣhipāmy ajasram aśhubhān āsurīṣhv eva yoniṣhu

sanskrit: असुरीं योनिमापन्ना मूढा जन्मनि जन्मनि।मामप्राप्यैव कौन्तेय ततो यान्त्यधमां गतिम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Cast into demoniacal wombs, deluded birth after birth, attaining not to Me, O Kaunteya, they sink into the lowest depths.
transliteration: āsurīṁ yonim āpannā mūḍhā janmani janmani mām aprāpyaiva kaunteya tato yānty adhamāṁ gatim

sanskrit: त्रिविधं नरकस्येदं द्वारं नाशनमात्मनः।कामः क्रोधस्तथा लोभस्तस्मादेतत्त्रयं त्यजेत् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Triple is the gate of this hell, destructive of the self—lust, wrath, and greed; therefore let man renounce these three.
transliteration: tri-vidhaṁ narakasyedaṁ dvāraṁ nāśhanam ātmanaḥ kāmaḥ krodhas tathā lobhas tasmād etat trayaṁ tyajet

sanskrit: एतैर्विमुक्तः कौन्तेय तमोद्वारैस्त्रिभिर्नरः।आचरत्यात्मनः श्रेयस्ततो याति परां गतिम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: A man liberated from these three gates of darkness, O son of Kunti, accomplisheth his own welfare, and thus reacheth the highest goal.
transliteration: etair vimuktaḥ kaunteya tamo-dvārais tribhir naraḥ ācharaty ātmanaḥ śhreyas tato yāti parāṁ gatim

sanskrit: यः शास्त्रविधिमुत्सृज्य वर्तते कामकारतः।न स सिद्धिमवाप्नोति न सुखं न परां गतिम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: He who, having cast aside the ordinances of the Scriptures, followeth the promptings of desire, attaineth not to perfection, nor happiness, nor the highest goal.
transliteration: yaḥ śhāstra-vidhim utsṛijya vartate kāma-kārataḥ na sa siddhim avāpnoti na sukhaṁ na parāṁ gatim

sanskrit: तस्माच्छास्त्रं प्रमाणं ते कार्याकार्यव्यवस्थितौ।ज्ञात्वा शास्त्रविधानोक्तं कर्म कर्तुमिहार्हसि ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Therefore let the Scriptures be thy authority, in determining what ought to be done, or what ought not to be done. Knowing what hath been declared by the ordinances of the Scriptures, thou oughtest to work in this world.
transliteration: tasmāch chhāstraṁ pramāṇaṁ te kāryākārya-vyavasthitau jñātvā śhāstra-vidhānoktaṁ karma kartum ihārhasi

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाचये शास्त्रविधिमुत्सृज्य यजन्ते श्रद्धयाऽन्विताः।तेषां निष्ठा तु का कृष्ण सत्त्वमाहो रजस्तमः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Arjuna said: Those that sacrifice full of faith, but casting aside the ordinances of the Scriptures, what is verily their condition, O Krishna? Is it one of Purity, Passion, or Darkness?
transliteration: arjuna uvācha ye śhāstra-vidhim utsṛijya yajante śhraddhayānvitāḥ teṣhāṁ niṣhṭhā tu kā kṛiṣhṇa sattvam āho rajas tamaḥ

sanskrit: श्री भगवानुवाचत्रिविधा भवति श्रद्धा देहिनां सा स्वभावजा।सात्त्विकी राजसी चैव तामसी चेति तां श्रृणु ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: The Blessed Lord said: Threefold is by nature the inborn faith of the embodied—pure, passionate, and dark. Hear thou of these.
transliteration: śhrī-bhagavān uvācha tri-vidhā bhavati śhraddhā dehināṁ sā svabhāva-jā sāttvikī rājasī chaiva tāmasī cheti tāṁ śhṛiṇu

sanskrit: सत्त्वानुरूपा सर्वस्य श्रद्धा भवति भारत।श्रद्धामयोऽयं पुरुषो यो यच्छ्रद्धः स एव सः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: The faith of each is shaped to his own nature, O Bhârata. The man consists of his faith; that which his faith is, he is even that.
transliteration: sattvānurūpā sarvasya śhraddhā bhavati bhārata śhraddhā-mayo ‘yaṁ puruṣho yo yach-chhraddhaḥ sa eva saḥ

sanskrit: यजन्ते सात्त्विका देवान्यक्षरक्षांसि राजसाः।प्रेतान्भूतगणांश्चान्ये यजन्ते तामसा जनाः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Pure men worship the Gods; the passionate the gnomes and giants; the others, the dark folk, worship ghosts and troops of nature-spirits.
transliteration: yajante sāttvikā devān yakṣha-rakṣhānsi rājasāḥ pretān bhūta-gaṇānśh chānye yajante tāmasā janāḥ

sanskrit: अशास्त्रविहितं घोरं तप्यन्ते ये तपो जनाः।दम्भाहङ्कारसंयुक्ताः कामरागबलान्विताः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: The men who perform severe austerities, unenjoined by the Scriptures, wedded to vanity and egoism, impelled by the force of their desires and passions,
transliteration: aśhāstra-vihitaṁ ghoraṁ tapyante ye tapo janāḥ dambhāhankāra-sanyuktāḥ kāma-rāga-balānvitāḥ

sanskrit: कर्षयन्तः शरीरस्थं भूतग्राममचेतसः।मां चैवान्तःशरीरस्थं तान्विद्ध्यासुरनिश्चयान् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Unintelligent, tormenting the aggregated elements forming the body, and Me also, seated in the inner body, know these demoniacal in their resolves.
transliteration: karṣhayantaḥ śharīra-sthaṁ bhūta-grāmam achetasaḥ māṁ chaivāntaḥ śharīra-sthaṁ tān viddhy āsura-niśhchayān

sanskrit: आहारस्त्वपि सर्वस्य त्रिविधो भवति प्रियः।यज्ञस्तपस्तथा दानं तेषां भेदमिमं श्रृणु ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: The food also which is dear to each is threefold, as also sacrifice, austerity and almsgiving. Hear thou the distinction of these.
transliteration: āhāras tv api sarvasya tri-vidho bhavati priyaḥ yajñas tapas tathā dānaṁ teṣhāṁ bhedam imaṁ śhṛiṇu

sanskrit: आयुःसत्त्वबलारोग्यसुखप्रीतिविवर्धनाः।रस्याः स्निग्धाः स्थिरा हृद्या आहाराः सात्त्विकप्रियाः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: The foods that augment vitality, energy, vigour, health, joy and cheerfulness, delicious, bland, substantial and agreeable, are dear to the pure.
transliteration: āyuḥ-sattva-balārogya-sukha-prīti-vivardhanāḥ rasyāḥ snigdhāḥ sthirā hṛidyā āhārāḥ sāttvika-priyāḥ

sanskrit: कट्वम्ललवणात्युष्णतीक्ष्णरूक्षविदाहिनः।आहारा राजसस्येष्टा दुःखशोकामयप्रदाः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: The passionate desire foods that are bitter, sour, saline, over-hot, pungent, dry and burning and which produce pain, grief and sickness.
transliteration: kaṭv-amla-lavaṇāty-uṣhṇa- tīkṣhṇa-rūkṣha-vidāhinaḥ āhārā rājasasyeṣhṭā duḥkha-śhokāmaya-pradāḥ

sanskrit: यातयामं गतरसं पूति पर्युषितं च यत्।उच्छिष्टमपि चामेध्यं भोजनं तामसप्रियम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: That which is stale and flat, putrid and corrupt, leavings also and unclean, is the food dear to the dark.
transliteration: yāta-yāmaṁ gata-rasaṁ pūti paryuṣhitaṁ cha yat uchchhiṣhṭam api chāmedhyaṁ bhojanaṁ tāmasa-priyam

sanskrit: अफलाकाङ्क्षिभिर्यज्ञो विधिदृष्टो य इज्यते।यष्टव्यमेवेति मनः समाधाय स सात्त्विकः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: The sacrifice which is offered by men without desire for fruit, as enjoined by the ordinances, under the firm belief that sacrifice is a duty, that is pure.
transliteration: aphalākāṅkṣhibhir yajño vidhi-driṣhṭo ya ijyate yaṣhṭavyam eveti manaḥ samādhāya sa sāttvikaḥ

sanskrit: अभिसंधाय तु फलं दम्भार्थमपि चैव यत्।इज्यते भरतश्रेष्ठ तं यज्ञं विद्धि राजसम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: The sacrifice offered with a view verily to fruit, and also indeed for self-glorification, O best of the Bhâratas; know thou that to be of passion.
transliteration: abhisandhāya tu phalaṁ dambhārtham api chaiva yat ijyate bharata-śhreṣhṭha taṁ yajñaṁ viddhi rājasam

sanskrit: विधिहीनमसृष्टान्नं मन्त्रहीनमदक्षिणम्।श्रद्धाविरहितं यज्ञं तामसं परिचक्षते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: The sacrifice contrary to the ordinances, without distributing food, devoid of words of power and without gifts, empty of faith, is said to be of darkness.
transliteration: vidhi-hīnam asṛiṣhṭānnaṁ mantra-hīnam adakṣhiṇam śhraddhā-virahitaṁ yajñaṁ tāmasaṁ parichakṣhate

sanskrit: देवद्विजगुरुप्राज्ञपूजनं शौचमार्जवम्।ब्रह्मचर्यमहिंसा च शारीरं तप उच्यते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Worship given to the Gods, to the twice-born, to the teachers and to the wise, purity, straightforwardness, continence and harmlessness, are called the austerity of the body.
transliteration: deva-dwija-guru-prājña- pūjanaṁ śhaucham ārjavam brahmacharyam ahinsā cha śhārīraṁ tapa uchyate

sanskrit: अनुद्वेगकरं वाक्यं सत्यं प्रियहितं च यत्।स्वाध्यायाभ्यसनं चैव वाङ्मयं तप उच्यते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Speech causing no annoyance, truthful, pleasant and beneficial, the practice of the study of the Scriptures, are called the austerity of speech.
transliteration: anudvega-karaṁ vākyaṁ satyaṁ priya-hitaṁ cha yat svādhyāyābhyasanaṁ chaiva vāṅ-mayaṁ tapa uchyate

sanskrit: मनःप्रसादः सौम्यत्वं मौनमात्मविनिग्रहः।भावसंशुद्धिरित्येतत्तपो मानसमुच्यते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Mental happiness, equilibrium, silence, self-control, purity of nature—these are called the austerity of the mind.
transliteration: manaḥ-prasādaḥ saumyatvaṁ maunam ātma-vinigrahaḥ bhāva-sanśhuddhir ity etat tapo mānasam uchyate

sanskrit: श्रद्धया परया तप्तं तपस्तत्ित्रविधं नरैः।अफलाकाङ्क्षिभिर्युक्तैः सात्त्विकं परिचक्षते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: This threefold austerity, performed by men with the utmost faith, without desire for fruit, harmonised, is said to be pure.
transliteration: śhraddhayā parayā taptaṁ tapas tat tri-vidhaṁ naraiḥ aphalākāṅkṣhibhir yuktaiḥ sāttvikaṁ parichakṣhate

sanskrit: सत्कारमानपूजार्थं तपो दम्भेन चैव यत्।क्रियते तदिह प्रोक्तं राजसं चलमध्रुवम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: The austerity which is practised with the object of gaining respect, honour and worship, and for ostentation, is said to be of passion, unstable and fleeting.
transliteration: satkāra-māna-pūjārthaṁ tapo dambhena chaiva yat kriyate tad iha proktaṁ rājasaṁ chalam adhruvam

sanskrit: मूढग्राहेणात्मनो यत्पीडया क्रियते तपः।परस्योत्सादनार्थं वा तत्तामसमुदाहृतम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: That austerity done under a deluded understanding, with self-torture, or with the object of destroying another, that is declared of darkness.
transliteration: mūḍha-grāheṇātmano yat pīḍayā kriyate tapaḥ parasyotsādanārthaṁ vā tat tāmasam udāhṛitam

sanskrit: दातव्यमिति यद्दानं दीयतेऽनुपकारिणे।देशे काले च पात्रे च तद्दानं सात्त्विकं स्मृतम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: That alms given to one who does nothing in return, believing that a gift ought to be made in a fit place and time to a worthy person, that alms is accounted pure.
transliteration: dātavyam iti yad dānaṁ dīyate ‘nupakāriṇe deśhe kāle cha pātre cha tad dānaṁ sāttvikaṁ smṛitam

sanskrit: यत्तु प्रत्युपकारार्थं फलमुद्दिश्य वा पुनः।दीयते च परिक्लिष्टं तद्दानं राजसं स्मृतम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: That given with a view to receiving in return, or looking for fruit again, or grudgingly, that alms is accounted of passion.
transliteration: yat tu pratyupakārārthaṁ phalam uddiśhya vā punaḥ dīyate cha parikliṣhṭaṁ tad dānaṁ rājasaṁ smṛitam

sanskrit: अदेशकाले यद्दानमपात्रेभ्यश्च दीयते।असत्कृतमवज्ञातं तत्तामसमुदाहृतम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: That alms given at unfit place and time, and to unworthy persons, disrespectfully and contemptuously, that is declared of darkness.
transliteration: adeśha-kāle yad dānam apātrebhyaśh cha dīyate asat-kṛitam avajñātaṁ tat tāmasam udāhṛitam

sanskrit: तत्सदिति निर्देशो ब्रह्मणस्त्रिविधः स्मृतः।ब्राह्मणास्तेन वेदाश्च यज्ञाश्च विहिताः पुरा ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: " Aum Tat Sat ," this has been considered to be the threefold designation of the Eternal . By that were ordained of old Brâhmanas, Vedas and sacrifices.
transliteration: oṁ tat sad iti nirdeśho brahmaṇas tri-vidhaḥ smṛitaḥ brāhmaṇās tena vedāśh cha yajñāśh cha vihitāḥ purā

sanskrit: तस्मादोमित्युदाहृत्य यज्ञदानतपःक्रियाः।प्रवर्तन्ते विधानोक्ताः सततं ब्रह्मवादिनाम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Therefore with the pronunciation of " Aum " the acts of sacrifice, gift and austerity as laid down in the ordinances are always commenced by the knowers of the Eternal .
transliteration: tasmād oṁ ity udāhṛitya yajña-dāna-tapaḥ-kriyāḥ pravartante vidhānoktāḥ satataṁ brahma-vādinām

sanskrit: तदित्यनभिसन्धाय फलं यज्ञतपःक्रियाः।दानक्रियाश्च विविधाः क्रियन्ते मोक्षकाङ्क्षि ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: With the pronunciation of " Tat " and without aiming at fruit are performed the various acts of sacrifice, austerity and gift, by those desiring liberation.
transliteration: tad ity anabhisandhāya phalaṁ yajña-tapaḥ-kriyāḥ dāna-kriyāśh cha vividhāḥ kriyante mokṣha-kāṅkṣhibhiḥ

sanskrit: सद्भावे साधुभावे च सदित्येतत्प्रयुज्यते।प्रशस्ते कर्मणि तथा सच्छब्दः पार्थ युज्यते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: " Sat " is used in the sense of reality and goodness; likewise, O Pârtha, the word " Sat " is used in the sense of a good work.
transliteration: sad-bhāve sādhu-bhāve cha sad ity etat prayujyate praśhaste karmaṇi tathā sach-chhabdaḥ pārtha yujyate

sanskrit: यज्ञे तपसि दाने च स्थितिः सदिति चोच्यते।कर्म चैव तदर्थीयं सदित्येवाभिधीयते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Steadfastness in sacrifice, austerity and gift is also called " Sat ", and action for the sake of the Supreme is also named " Sat ."
transliteration: yajñe tapasi dāne cha sthitiḥ sad iti chochyate karma chaiva tad-arthīyaṁ sad ity evābhidhīyate

sanskrit: अश्रद्धया हुतं दत्तं तपस्तप्तं कृतं च यत्।असदित्युच्यते पार्थ न च तत्प्रेत्य नो इह ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Whatsoever is wrought without faith, oblation, gift, austerity, or other deed, "Asat" it is called, O Pârtha; it is nought, here or hereafter.
transliteration: aśhraddhayā hutaṁ dattaṁ tapas taptaṁ kṛitaṁ cha yat asad ity uchyate pārtha na cha tat pretya no iha

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच संन्यासस्य महाबाहो तत्त्वमिच्छामि वेदितुम्। त्यागस्य च हृषीकेश पृथक्केशिनिषूदन ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Arjuna said: I desire, O mighty-armed, to know severally the essence of renunciation, O Hrishîkesha, and of relinquishment, O Keshinishûdana.
transliteration: arjuna uvācha sannyāsasya mahā-bāho tattvam ichchhāmi veditum tyāgasya cha hṛiṣhīkeśha pṛithak keśhi-niṣhūdana

sanskrit: श्री भगवानुवाच काम्यानां कर्मणां न्यासं संन्यासं कवयो विदुः। सर्वकर्मफलत्यागं प्राहुस्त्यागं विचक्षणाः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: The Blessed Lord said: Sages have known as renunciation the renouncing of works with desire; the relinquishing of the fruit of all actions is called relinquishment by the wise.
transliteration: śhrī-bhagavān uvācha kāmyānāṁ karmaṇāṁ nyāsaṁ sannyāsaṁ kavayo viduḥ sarva-karma-phala-tyāgaṁ prāhus tyāgaṁ vichakṣhaṇāḥ

sanskrit: त्याज्यं दोषवदित्येके कर्म प्राहुर्मनीषिणः। यज्ञदानतपःकर्म न त्याज्यमिति चापरे ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: "Action should be relinquished as an evil," declare some thoughtful men; "acts of sacrifice, gift and austerity should not be relinquished," say others.
transliteration: tyājyaṁ doṣha-vad ity eke karma prāhur manīṣhiṇaḥ yajña-dāna-tapaḥ-karma na tyājyam iti chāpare

sanskrit: निश्चयं श्रृणु मे तत्र त्यागे भरतसत्तम।त्यागो हि पुरुषव्याघ्र त्रिविधः संप्रकीर्तितः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Hear my conclusions as to that relinquishment, O best of the Bhâratas; since relinquishment, O tiger of men, has been explained as threefold.
transliteration: niśhchayaṁ śhṛiṇu me tatra tyāge bharata-sattama tyāgo hi puruṣha-vyāghra tri-vidhaḥ samprakīrtitaḥ

sanskrit: यज्ञदानतपःकर्म न त्याज्यं कार्यमेव तत्।यज्ञो दानं तपश्चैव पावनानि मनीषिणाम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Acts of sacrifice, gift and austerity should not be relinquished, but should be performed; sacrifice, gift and also austerity are the purifiers of the intelligent.
transliteration: yajña-dāna-tapaḥ-karma na tyājyaṁ kāryam eva tat yajño dānaṁ tapaśh chaiva pāvanāni manīṣhiṇām

sanskrit: एतान्यपि तु कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा फलानि च।कर्तव्यानीति मे पार्थ निश्िचतं मतमुत्तमम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: But even these actions should be done leaving aside attachment and fruit, O Pârtha; that is my certain and best belief.
transliteration: etāny api tu karmāṇi saṅgaṁ tyaktvā phalāni cha kartavyānīti me pārtha niśhchitaṁ matam uttamam

sanskrit: नियतस्य तु संन्यासः कर्मणो नोपपद्यते।मोहात्तस्य परित्यागस्तामसः परिकीर्तितः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Verily renunciation of actions that are prescribed is not proper; the relinquishment thereof from delusion is said to be of darkness.
transliteration: niyatasya tu sannyāsaḥ karmaṇo nopapadyate mohāt tasya parityāgas tāmasaḥ parikīrtitaḥ

sanskrit: दुःखमित्येव यत्कर्म कायक्लेशभयात्त्यजेत्।स कृत्वा राजसं त्यागं नैव त्यागफलं लभेत् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: He who relinquisheth an action from fear of physical suffering, saying "Painful," thus performing a passionate relinquishment, obtaineth not the fruit of relinquishment.
transliteration: duḥkham ity eva yat karma kāya-kleśha-bhayāt tyajet sa kṛitvā rājasaṁ tyāgaṁ naiva tyāga-phalaṁ labhet

sanskrit: कार्यमित्येव यत्कर्म नियतं क्रियतेऽर्जुन।सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा फलं चैव स त्यागः सात्त्विको मतः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: He who performeth a prescribed action, saying, "it ought to be done," O Arjuna, relinquishing attachment and also fruit, that relinquishment is regarded as pure.
transliteration: kāryam ity eva yat karma niyataṁ kriyate ‘rjuna saṅgaṁ tyaktvā phalaṁ chaiva sa tyāgaḥ sāttviko mataḥ

sanskrit: न द्वेष्ट्यकुशलं कर्म कुशले नानुषज्जते।त्यागी सत्त्वसमाविष्टो मेधावी छिन्नसंशयः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: The relinquisher pervaded by purity, intelligent and with doubts cut away, hateth not unpleasurable action nor is attached to pleasurable.
transliteration: na dveṣhṭy akuśhalaṁ karma kuśhale nānuṣhajjate tyāgī sattva-samāviṣhṭo medhāvī chhinna-sanśhayaḥ

sanskrit: न हि देहभृता शक्यं त्यक्तुं कर्माण्यशेषतः।यस्तु कर्मफलत्यागी स त्यागीत्यभिधीयते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
