text_en: Nor indeed can embodied beings completely relinquish action; verily he who relinquisheth the fruit of action, he is said to be a relinquisher.
transliteration: na hi deha-bhṛitā śhakyaṁ tyaktuṁ karmāṇy aśheṣhataḥ yas tu karma-phala-tyāgī sa tyāgīty abhidhīyate

sanskrit: अनिष्टमिष्टं मिश्रं च त्रिविधं कर्मणः फलम्।भवत्यत्यागिनां प्रेत्य न तु संन्यासिनां क्वचित् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Good, evil and mixed—threefold is the fruit of action hereafter for the non-relinquisher; but there is none ever for the renouncer.
transliteration: aniṣhṭam iṣhṭaṁ miśhraṁ cha tri-vidhaṁ karmaṇaḥ phalam bhavaty atyāgināṁ pretya na tu sannyāsināṁ kvachit

sanskrit: पञ्चैतानि महाबाहो कारणानि निबोध मे।सांख्ये कृतान्ते प्रोक्तानि सिद्धये सर्वकर्मणाम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: These five causes, O mighty-armed, learn of Me as declared in the Sâñkhya system for the accomplishment of all actions:
transliteration: pañchaitāni mahā-bāho kāraṇāni nibodha me sānkhye kṛitānte proktāni siddhaye sarva-karmaṇām

sanskrit: अधिष्ठानं तथा कर्ता करणं च पृथग्विधम्।विविधाश्च पृथक्चेष्टा दैवं चैवात्र पञ्चमम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: The body, the actor, the various organs, the diverse kinds of energies, and the presiding deities also, the fifth.
transliteration: adhiṣhṭhānaṁ tathā kartā karaṇaṁ cha pṛithag-vidham vividhāśh cha pṛithak cheṣhṭā daivaṁ chaivātra pañchamam

sanskrit: शरीरवाङ्मनोभिर्यत्कर्म प्रारभते नरः।न्याय्यं वा विपरीतं वा पञ्चैते तस्य हेतवः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Whatever action a man performeth by his body, speech and mind, whether right or the reverse, these five are the cause thereof.
transliteration: śharīra-vāṅ-manobhir yat karma prārabhate naraḥ nyāyyaṁ vā viparītaṁ vā pañchaite tasya hetavaḥ

sanskrit: तत्रैवं सति कर्तारमात्मानं केवलं तु यः।पश्यत्यकृतबुद्धित्वान्न स पश्यति दुर्मतिः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: That being so, he verily who—owing to untrained Reason—looketh on his Self , which is isolated, as the actor, he of perverted intelligence, seeth not.
transliteration: tatraivaṁ sati kartāram ātmānaṁ kevalaṁ tu yaḥ paśhyaty akṛita-buddhitvān na sa paśhyati durmatiḥ

sanskrit: यस्य नाहंकृतो भावो बुद्धिर्यस्य न लिप्यते।हत्वापि स इमाँल्लोकान्न हन्ति न निबध्यते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: He who is free from the egoistic notion, whose Reason is not affected, though he slay these peoples, he slayeth not, nor is bound.
transliteration: yasya nāhankṛito bhāvo buddhir yasya na lipyate hatvā ‘pi sa imāl lokān na hanti na nibadhyate

sanskrit: ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं परिज्ञाता त्रिविधा कर्मचोदना।करणं कर्म कर्तेति त्रिविधः कर्मसंग्रहः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Knowledge, the knowable and the knower, the threefold impulse to action; the organ, the action, the actor, the threefold constituents of action.
transliteration: jñānaṁ jñeyaṁ parijñātā tri-vidhā karma-chodanā karaṇaṁ karma karteti tri-vidhaḥ karma-saṅgrahaḥ

sanskrit: ज्ञानं कर्म च कर्ता च त्रिधैव गुणभेदतः।प्रोच्यते गुणसंख्याने यथावच्छृणु तान्यपि ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Knowledge, action and actor in the category of qualities are also said to be severally threefold, from the difference of qualities; hear thou duly these also.
transliteration: jñānaṁ karma cha kartā cha tridhaiva guṇa-bhedataḥ prochyate guṇa-saṅkhyāne yathāvach chhṛiṇu tāny api

sanskrit: सर्वभूतेषु येनैकं भावमव्ययमीक्षते।अविभक्तं विभक्तेषु तज्ज्ञानं विद्धि सात्त्विकम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: That by which one indestructible Being is seen in all beings, inseparate in the separated, know thou that knowledge as pure.
transliteration: sarva-bhūteṣhu yenaikaṁ bhāvam avyayam īkṣhate avibhaktaṁ vibhakteṣhu taj jñānaṁ viddhi sāttvikam

sanskrit: पृथक्त्वेन तु यज्ज्ञानं नानाभावान्पृथग्विधान्।वेत्ति सर्वेषु भूतेषु तज्ज्ञानं विद्धि राजसम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: But that knowledge which regardeth the several manifold existences in all beings as separate, that knowledge know thou as of passion;
transliteration: pṛithaktvena tu yaj jñānaṁ nānā-bhāvān pṛithag-vidhān vetti sarveṣhu bhūteṣhu taj jñānaṁ viddhi rājasam

sanskrit: यत्तु कृत्स्नवदेकस्मिन्कार्ये सक्तमहैतुकम्।अतत्त्वार्थवदल्पं च तत्तामसमुदाहृतम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: While that which clingeth to each one thing as if it were the whole, without reason, without grasping the reality, narrow, that is declared to be dark.
transliteration: yat tu kṛitsna-vad ekasmin kārye saktam ahaitukam atattvārtha-vad alpaṁ cha tat tāmasam udāhṛitam

sanskrit: नियतं सङ्गरहितमरागद्वेषतः कृतम्।अफलप्रेप्सुना कर्म यत्तत्सात्त्विकमुच्यते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: An action which is ordained, done by one undesirous of fruit, devoid of attachment, without love or hate, that is called pure.
transliteration: niyataṁ saṅga-rahitam arāga-dveṣhataḥ kṛitam aphala-prepsunā karma yat tat sāttvikam uchyate

sanskrit: यत्तु कामेप्सुना कर्म साहङ्कारेण वा पुनः।क्रियते बहुलायासं तद्राजसमुदाहृतम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: But that action that is done by one longing for desires, or again with egoism, or with much effort, that is declared to be passionate.
transliteration: yat tu kāmepsunā karma sāhankāreṇa vā punaḥ kriyate bahulāyāsaṁ tad rājasam udāhṛitam

sanskrit: अनुबन्धं क्षयं हिंसामनपेक्ष्य च पौरुषम्।मोहादारभ्यते कर्म यत्तत्तामसमुच्यते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: The action undertaken from delusion, without regard to capacity and consequences—loss and injury to others—that is declared to be dark.
transliteration: anubandhaṁ kṣhayaṁ hinsām anapekṣhya cha pauruṣham mohād ārabhyate karma yat tat tāmasam uchyate

sanskrit: मुक्तसङ्गोऽनहंवादी धृत्युत्साहसमन्वितः।सिद्ध्यसिद्ध्योर्निर्विकारः कर्ता सात्त्विक उच्यते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Liberated from attachment, not egoistic, endued with firmness and confidence, unchanged by success or failure, that actor is called pure.
transliteration: mukta-saṅgo ‘nahaṁ-vādī dhṛity-utsāha-samanvitaḥ siddhy-asiddhyor nirvikāraḥ kartā sāttvika uchyate

sanskrit: रागी कर्मफलप्रेप्सुर्लुब्धो हिंसात्मकोऽशुचिः।हर्षशोकान्वितः कर्ता राजसः परिकीर्तितः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Impassioned, desiring to obtain the fruit of actions, greedy, harmful, impure, moved by joy and sorrow, such an actor is pronounced passionate.
transliteration: rāgī karma-phala-prepsur lubdho hinsātmako ‘śhuchiḥ harṣha-śhokānvitaḥ kartā rājasaḥ parikīrtitaḥ

sanskrit: अयुक्तः प्राकृतः स्तब्धः शठो नैष्कृतिकोऽलसः।विषादी दीर्घसूत्री च कर्ता तामस उच्यते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Discordant, vulgar, stubborn, cheating, malicious, indolent, despairful, procrastinating, that actor is called dark.
transliteration: ayuktaḥ prākṛitaḥ stabdhaḥ śhaṭho naiṣhkṛitiko ‘lasaḥ viṣhādī dīrgha-sūtrī cha kartā tāmasa uchyate

sanskrit: बुद्धेर्भेदं धृतेश्चैव गुणतस्त्रिविधं श्रृणु।प्रोच्यमानमशेषेण पृथक्त्वेन धनञ्जय ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: The division of Reason and of firmness also, threefold according to the qualities, hear thou related, unreservedly and severally, O Dhananjaya.
transliteration: buddher bhedaṁ dhṛiteśh chaiva guṇatas tri-vidhaṁ śhṛiṇu prochyamānam aśheṣheṇa pṛithaktvena dhanañjaya

sanskrit: प्रवृत्तिं च निवृत्तिं च कार्याकार्ये भयाभये।बन्धं मोक्षं च या वेत्ति बुद्धिः सा पार्थ सात्त्विकी ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: That which knoweth energy and abstinence, what ought to be done and what ought not to be done, fear and fearlessness, bondage and liberation, that Reason is pure, O Pârtha.
transliteration: pravṛittiṁ cha nivṛittiṁ cha kāryākārye bhayābhaye bandhaṁ mokṣhaṁ cha yā vetti buddhiḥ sā pārtha sāttvikī

sanskrit: यया धर्ममधर्मं च कार्यं चाकार्यमेव च।अयथावत्प्रजानाति बुद्धिः सा पार्थ राजसी ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: That by which one understandeth away Right and Wrong and also what ought to be done and what ought not to be done, that Reason, O Pârtha, is passionate.
transliteration: yayā dharmam adharmaṁ cha kāryaṁ chākāryam eva cha ayathāvat prajānāti buddhiḥ sā pārtha rājasī

sanskrit: अधर्मं धर्ममिति या मन्यते तमसाऽऽवृता।सर्वार्थान्विपरीतांश्च बुद्धिः सा पार्थ तामसी ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: That which, enwrapped in darkness, thinketh wrong to be right and seeth all things subverted, that Reason, O Pârtha, is of darkness.
transliteration: adharmaṁ dharmam iti yā manyate tamasāvṛitā sarvārthān viparītānśh cha buddhiḥ sā pārtha tāmasī

sanskrit: धृत्या यया धारयते मनःप्राणेन्द्रियक्रियाः।योगेनाव्यभिचारिण्या धृतिः सा पार्थ सात्त्विकी ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
transliteration: dhṛityā yayā dhārayate manaḥ-prāṇendriya-kriyāḥ yogenāvyabhichāriṇyā dhṛitiḥ sā pārtha sāttvikī

sanskrit: यया तु धर्मकामार्थान् धृत्या धारयतेऽर्जुन।प्रसङ्गेन फलाकाङ्क्षी धृतिः सा पार्थ राजसी ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: But the firmness, O Arjuna, by which, from attachment desirous of fruit, one holdeth fast duty, desire and wealth, that firmness, O Pârtha, is passionate.
transliteration: yayā tu dharma-kāmārthān dhṛityā dhārayate ‘rjuna prasaṅgena phalākāṅkṣhī dhṛitiḥ sā pārtha rājasī

sanskrit: यया स्वप्नं भयं शोकं विषादं मदमेव च।न विमुञ्चति दुर्मेधा धृतिः सा पार्थ तामसी ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: That by which one from stupidity doth not abandon sleep, fear, grief, despair, and also vanity, that firmness, O Pârtha, is dark.
transliteration: yayā svapnaṁ bhayaṁ śhokaṁ viṣhādaṁ madam eva cha na vimuñchati durmedhā dhṛitiḥ sā pārtha tāmasī

sanskrit: सुखं त्विदानीं त्रिविधं श्रृणु मे भरतर्षभ।अभ्यासाद्रमते यत्र दुःखान्तं च निगच्छति ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: And now the threefold kinds of pleasure hear thou from Me, O bull of the Bhâratas. That in which one by practice rejoiceth, and which putteth an end to pain;
transliteration: sukhaṁ tv idānīṁ tri-vidhaṁ śhṛiṇu me bharatarṣhabha abhyāsād ramate yatra duḥkhāntaṁ cha nigachchhati yat tad agre viṣham iva pariṇāme ‘mṛitopamam tat sukhaṁ sāttvikaṁ proktam ātma-buddhi-prasāda-jam

sanskrit: यत्तदग्रे विषमिव परिणामेऽमृतोपमम्।तत्सुखं सात्त्विकं प्रोक्तमात्मबुद्धिप्रसादजम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Which at first is as venom but in the end is as nectar; that pleasure is said to be pure, born of the blissful knowledge of the Self .
transliteration: yat tad agre viṣam iva pariṇāme 'mṛtopamam tat sukhaṁ sāttvikaṁ proktam ātma-buddhi-prasāda-jam

sanskrit: विषयेन्द्रियसंयोगाद्यत्तदग्रेऽमृतोपमम्।परिणामे विषमिव तत्सुखं राजसं स्मृतम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: That which from the union of the senses with their objects at first is as nectar, but in the end is like venom, that pleasure is accounted passionate.
transliteration: viṣhayendriya-sanyogād yat tad agre ’mṛitopamam pariṇāme viṣham iva tat sukhaṁ rājasaṁ smṛitam

sanskrit: यदग्रे चानुबन्धे च सुखं मोहनमात्मनः।निद्रालस्यप्रमादोत्थं तत्तामसमुदाहृतम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: That pleasure which both at first and afterwards is delusive of the self, arising from sleep, indolence and heedlessness, that is declared dark.
transliteration: yad agre chānubandhe cha sukhaṁ mohanam ātmanaḥ nidrālasya-pramādotthaṁ tat tāmasam udāhṛitam

sanskrit: न तदस्ति पृथिव्यां वा दिवि देवेषु वा पुनः।सत्त्वं प्रकृतिजैर्मुक्तं यदेभिः स्यात्ित्रभिर्गुणैः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: There is not an entity, either on the earth or again in heaven among the Shining Ones, that is liberated from these three qualities, born of Matter.
transliteration: na tad asti pṛithivyāṁ vā divi deveṣhu vā punaḥ sattvaṁ prakṛiti-jair muktaṁ yad ebhiḥ syāt tribhir guṇaiḥ

sanskrit: ब्राह्मणक्षत्रियविशां शूद्राणां च परंतप।कर्माणि प्रविभक्तानि स्वभावप्रभवैर्गुणैः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Of Brâhmanas, Kshattriyas, Vaishyas and Shûdras, O Parantapa, the duties have been distributed, according to the qualities born of their own natures.
transliteration: brāhmaṇa-kṣhatriya-viśhāṁ śhūdrāṇāṁ cha parantapa karmāṇi pravibhaktāni svabhāva-prabhavair guṇaiḥ

sanskrit: शमो दमस्तपः शौचं क्षान्तिरार्जवमेव च।ज्ञानं विज्ञानमास्तिक्यं ब्रह्मकर्म स्वभावजम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Serenity, self-restraint, austerity, purity, forgiveness and also uprightness, wisdom, knowledge, belief in God, are the Brâhmana duty, born of his own nature.
transliteration: śhamo damas tapaḥ śhauchaṁ kṣhāntir ārjavam eva cha jñānaṁ vijñānam āstikyaṁ brahma-karma svabhāva-jam

sanskrit: शौर्यं तेजो धृतिर्दाक्ष्यं युद्धे चाप्यपलायनम्।दानमीश्वरभावश्च क्षात्रं कर्म स्वभावजम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Prowess, splendour, firmness, dexterity, and also not flying from battle, generosity, the nature of a ruler, are the Kshattriya duty, born of his own nature.
transliteration: śhauryaṁ tejo dhṛitir dākṣhyaṁ yuddhe chāpy apalāyanam dānam īśhvara-bhāvaśh cha kṣhātraṁ karma svabhāva-jam

sanskrit: कृषिगौरक्ष्यवाणिज्यं वैश्यकर्म स्वभावजम्।परिचर्यात्मकं कर्म शूद्रस्यापि स्वभावजम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Ploughing, protection of kine, and trade are the Vaishya duty, born of his own nature. Action of the nature of service is the Shûdra duty, born of his own nature.
transliteration: kṛiṣhi-gau-rakṣhya-vāṇijyaṁ vaiśhya-karma svabhāva-jam paricharyātmakaṁ karma śhūdrasyāpi svabhāva-jam

sanskrit: स्वे स्वे कर्मण्यभिरतः संसिद्धिं लभते नरः।स्वकर्मनिरतः सिद्धिं यथा विन्दति तच्छृणु ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Man reacheth perfection by each being intent on his own duty. Listen thou how perfection is won by him who is intent on his own duty.
transliteration: sve sve karmaṇy abhirataḥ sansiddhiṁ labhate naraḥ sva-karma-nirataḥ siddhiṁ yathā vindati tach chhṛiṇu

sanskrit: यतः प्रवृत्तिर्भूतानां येन सर्वमिदं ततम्।स्वकर्मणा तमभ्यर्च्य सिद्धिं विन्दति मानवः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: He from whom is the emanation of beings, by Whom all This is pervaded, by worshipping Him in his own duty a man winneth perfection.
transliteration: yataḥ pravṛittir bhūtānāṁ yena sarvam idaṁ tatam sva-karmaṇā tam abhyarchya siddhiṁ vindati mānavaḥ

sanskrit: श्रेयान्स्वधर्मो विगुणः परधर्मात्स्वनुष्ठितात्।स्वभावनियतं कर्म कुर्वन्नाप्नोति किल्बिषम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Better is one's own duty though destitute of merits than the well-executed duty of another. He who doeth the duty laid down by his own nature incurreth not sin.
transliteration: śhreyān swa-dharmo viguṇaḥ para-dharmāt sv-anuṣhṭhitāt svabhāva-niyataṁ karma kurvan nāpnoti kilbiṣham

sanskrit: सहजं कर्म कौन्तेय सदोषमपि न त्यजेत्।सर्वारम्भा हि दोषेण धूमेनाग्निरिवावृताः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Congenital duty, O son of Kunti, though defective, ought not to be abandoned. All undertakings indeed are clouded by defects as fire by smoke.
transliteration: saha-jaṁ karma kaunteya sa-doṣham api na tyajet sarvārambhā hi doṣheṇa dhūmenāgnir ivāvṛitāḥ

sanskrit: असक्तबुद्धिः सर्वत्र जितात्मा विगतस्पृहः।नैष्कर्म्यसिद्धिं परमां संन्यासेनाधिगच्छति ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: He whose Reason is everywhere unattached, the self-subdued, dead to desires, he goeth by renunciation to the supreme perfection of freedom from obligation.
transliteration: asakta-buddhiḥ sarvatra jitātmā vigata-spṛihaḥ naiṣhkarmya-siddhiṁ paramāṁ sannyāsenādhigachchhati

sanskrit: सिद्धिं प्राप्तो यथा ब्रह्म तथाप्नोति निबोध मे।समासेनैव कौन्तेय निष्ठा ज्ञानस्य या परा ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: How he who hath attained perfection obtaineth the Eternal , that highest state of wisdom, learn thou from Me only succinctly, O Kaunteya.
transliteration: siddhiṁ prāpto yathā brahma tathāpnoti nibodha me samāsenaiva kaunteya niṣhṭhā jñānasya yā parā

sanskrit: बुद्ध्या विशुद्धया युक्तो धृत्याऽऽत्मानं नियम्य च।शब्दादीन् विषयांस्त्यक्त्वा रागद्वेषौ व्युदस्य च ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: United to this Reason, purified, controlling the self by firmness, having abandoned sound and the other objects of the senses, having laid aside passion and malice,
transliteration: buddhyā viśhuddhayā yukto dhṛityātmānaṁ niyamya cha śhabdādīn viṣhayāns tyaktvā rāga-dveṣhau vyudasya cha

sanskrit: विविक्तसेवी लघ्वाशी यतवाक्कायमानसः।ध्यानयोगपरो नित्यं वैराग्यं समुपाश्रितः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Dwelling in solitude, abstemious, speech, body and mind subdued, constantly fixed in meditation and yoga, taking refuge in dispassion,
transliteration: vivikta-sevī laghv-āśhī yata-vāk-kāya-mānasaḥ dhyāna-yoga-paro nityaṁ vairāgyaṁ samupāśhritaḥ

sanskrit: अहङ्कारं बलं दर्पं कामं क्रोधं परिग्रहम्।विमुच्य निर्ममः शान्तो ब्रह्मभूयाय कल्पते ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Having cast aside egoism, violence, arrogance, desire, wrath, covetousness, selfless and peaceful—he is fit to become the Eternal .
transliteration: ahankāraṁ balaṁ darpaṁ kāmaṁ krodhaṁ parigraham vimuchya nirmamaḥ śhānto brahma-bhūyāya kalpate

sanskrit: ब्रह्मभूतः प्रसन्नात्मा न शोचति न काङ्क्षति।समः सर्वेषु भूतेषु मद्भक्तिं लभते पराम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Becoming the Eternal , serene in the Self , he neither grieveth nor desireth; the same to all beings, he obtaineth supreme devotion unto Me.
transliteration: brahma-bhūtaḥ prasannātmā na śhochati na kāṅkṣhati samaḥ sarveṣhu bhūteṣhu mad-bhaktiṁ labhate parām

sanskrit: भक्त्या मामभिजानाति यावान्यश्चास्मि तत्त्वतः।ततो मां तत्त्वतो ज्ञात्वा विशते तदनन्तरम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: By devotion he knoweth Me in essence, who and what I am; having thus known Me in essence he forthwith entereth into the Supreme.
transliteration: bhaktyā mām abhijānāti yāvān yaśh chāsmi tattvataḥ tato māṁ tattvato jñātvā viśhate tad-anantaram

sanskrit: सर्वकर्माण्यपि सदा कुर्वाणो मद्व्यपाश्रयः।मत्प्रसादादवाप्नोति शाश्वतं पदमव्ययम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Though ever performing all actions, taking refuge in Me, by My grace he obtaineth the eternal indestructible abode.
transliteration: sarva-karmāṇy api sadā kurvāṇo mad-vyapāśhrayaḥ mat-prasādād avāpnoti śhāśhvataṁ padam avyayam

sanskrit: चेतसा सर्वकर्माणि मयि संन्यस्य मत्परः।बुद्धियोगमुपाश्रित्य मच्चित्तः सततं भव ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Renouncing mentally all works in Me, intent on Me, resorting to the yoga of discrimination, have thy thought ever on Me.
transliteration: chetasā sarva-karmāṇi mayi sannyasya mat-paraḥ buddhi-yogam upāśhritya mach-chittaḥ satataṁ bhava

sanskrit: मच्चित्तः सर्वदुर्गाणि मत्प्रसादात्तरिष्यसि।अथ चेत्त्वमहङ्कारान्न श्रोष्यसि विनङ्क्ष्यसि ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Thinking on Me, thou shalt overcome all obstacles by My grace: but if from egoism thou wilt not listen, thou shalt be destroyed utterly.
transliteration: mach-chittaḥ sarva-durgāṇi mat-prasādāt tariṣhyasi atha chet tvam ahankārān na śhroṣhyasi vinaṅkṣhyasi

sanskrit: यदहङ्कारमाश्रित्य न योत्स्य इति मन्यसे।मिथ्यैष व्यवसायस्ते प्रकृतिस्त्वां नियोक्ष्यति ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Entrenched in egoism, thou thinkest, "I will not fight;" to no purpose thy determination; nature will constrain thee.
transliteration: yad ahankāram āśhritya na yotsya iti manyase mithyaiṣha vyavasāyas te prakṛitis tvāṁ niyokṣhyati

sanskrit: स्वभावजेन कौन्तेय निबद्धः स्वेन कर्मणा।कर्तुं नेच्छसि यन्मोहात्करिष्यस्यवशोऽपि तत् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: O son of Kuntî, bound by thine own duty born of thine own nature, that which from delusion thou desirest not to do, even that helplessly thou shalt perform.
transliteration: swbhāva-jena kaunteya nibaddhaḥ svena karmaṇā kartuṁ nechchhasi yan mohāt kariṣhyasy avaśho ’pi tat

sanskrit: ईश्वरः सर्वभूतानां हृद्देशेऽर्जुन तिष्ठति।भ्रामयन्सर्वभूतानि यन्त्रारूढानि मायया ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: The Lord dwelleth in the hearts of all beings, O Arjuna, by His illusive Power causing all beings to revolve, as though mounted on a potter's wheel.
transliteration: īśhvaraḥ sarva-bhūtānāṁ hṛid-deśhe ‘rjuna tiṣhṭhati bhrāmayan sarva-bhūtāni yantrārūḍhāni māyayā

sanskrit: तमेव शरणं गच्छ सर्वभावेन भारत।तत्प्रसादात्परां शान्तिं स्थानं प्राप्स्यसि शाश्वतम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Flee unto Him for shelter with all thy being, O Bhârata; by His grace thou shalt obtain supreme peace, the everlasting dwelling-place.
transliteration: tam eva śharaṇaṁ gachchha sarva-bhāvena bhārata tat-prasādāt parāṁ śhāntiṁ sthānaṁ prāpsyasi śhāśhvatam

sanskrit: इति ते ज्ञानमाख्यातं गुह्याद्गुह्यतरं मया।विमृश्यैतदशेषेण यथेच्छसि तथा कुरु ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Thus hath wisdom, more secret than secrecy itself, been declared unto thee by Me; having reflected on it fully, then act thou as thou listest.
transliteration: iti te jñānam ākhyātaṁ guhyād guhyataraṁ mayā vimṛiśhyaitad aśheṣheṇa yathechchhasi tathā kuru

sanskrit: सर्वगुह्यतमं भूयः श्रृणु मे परमं वचः।इष्टोऽसि मे दृढमिति ततो वक्ष्यामि ते हितम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Listen thou again to My supreme word, most secret of all; beloved art thou of Me, and steadfast of heart, therefore will I speak for thy benefit.
transliteration: sarva-guhyatamaṁ bhūyaḥ śhṛiṇu me paramaṁ vachaḥ iṣhṭo ‘si me dṛiḍham iti tato vakṣhyāmi te hitam

sanskrit: मन्मना भव मद्भक्तो मद्याजी मां नमस्कुरु।मामेवैष्यसि सत्यं ते प्रतिजाने प्रियोऽसि मे ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Merge thy mind in Me, be my devotee, sacrifice to Me, prostrate thyself before Me, thou shalt come even to Me. I pledge thee My troth; thou art dear to Me.
transliteration: man-manā bhava mad-bhakto mad-yājī māṁ namaskuru mām evaiṣhyasi satyaṁ te pratijāne priyo ‘si me

sanskrit: सर्वधर्मान्परित्यज्य मामेकं शरणं व्रज।अहं त्वा सर्वपापेभ्यो मोक्षयिष्यामि मा शुचः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Abandoning all duties come unto Me alone for shelter; sorrow not, I will liberate thee from all sins.
transliteration: sarva-dharmān parityajya mām ekaṁ śharaṇaṁ vraja ahaṁ tvāṁ sarva-pāpebhyo mokṣhayiṣhyāmi mā śhuchaḥ

sanskrit: इदं ते नातपस्काय नाभक्ताय कदाचन।न चाशुश्रूषवे वाच्यं न च मां योऽभ्यसूयति ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Never is this to be spoken by thee to anyone who is without asceticism, nor without devotion, nor to one who desireth not to listen, nor yet to him who speaketh evil of Me.
transliteration: idaṁ te nātapaskyāya nābhaktāya kadāchana na chāśhuśhruṣhave vāchyaṁ na cha māṁ yo ‘bhyasūtayi

sanskrit: य इमं परमं गुह्यं मद्भक्तेष्वभिधास्यति।भक्ितं मयि परां कृत्वा मामेवैष्यत्यसंशयः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: He who shall declare this supreme secret among My devotees, having shown the highest devotion for Me, without doubt he shall come to Me.
transliteration: ya idaṁ paramaṁ guhyaṁ mad-bhakteṣhv abhidhāsyati bhaktiṁ mayi parāṁ kṛitvā mām evaiṣhyaty asanśhayaḥ

sanskrit: न च तस्मान्मनुष्येषु कश्िचन्मे प्रियकृत्तमः।भविता न च मे तस्मादन्यः प्रियतरो भुवि ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Nor is there any among men who performeth dearer service to Me than he, nor any other shall be more beloved by Me on earth than he.
transliteration: na cha tasmān manuṣhyeṣhu kaśhchin me priya-kṛittamaḥ bhavitā na cha me tasmād anyaḥ priyataro bhuvi

sanskrit: अध्येष्यते च य इमं धर्म्यं संवादमावयोः।ज्ञानयज्ञेन तेनाहमिष्टः स्यामिति मे मतिः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: And he who shall study this sacred dialogue of ours, by him I shall be worshipped with the sacrifice of wisdom. Such is My mind.
transliteration: adhyeṣhyate cha ya imaṁ dharmyaṁ saṁvādam āvayoḥ jñāna-yajñena tenāham iṣhṭaḥ syām iti me matiḥ

sanskrit: श्रद्धावाननसूयश्च श्रृणुयादपि यो नरः।सोऽपि मुक्तः शुभाँल्लोकान्प्राप्नुयात्पुण्यकर्मणाम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: The man also who, full of faith, merely heareth it unreviling, even he, freed from evil, obtaineth the radiant worlds of the righteous.
transliteration: śhraddhāvān anasūyaśh cha śhṛiṇuyād api yo naraḥ so ‘pi muktaḥ śhubhāl lokān prāpnuyāt puṇya-karmaṇām

sanskrit: कच्चिदेतच्छ्रुतं पार्थ त्वयैकाग्रेण चेतसा।कच्चिदज्ञानसंमोहः प्रनष्टस्ते धनञ्जय ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Hath this been heard, O son of Prithâ, with one-pointed mind? Has thy delusion, caused by unwisdom, been destroyed, O Dhananjaya?
transliteration: kachchid etach chhrutaṁ pārtha tvayaikāgreṇa chetasā kachchid ajñāna-sammohaḥ pranaṣhṭas te dhanañjaya

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाचनष्टो मोहः स्मृतिर्लब्धा त्वत्प्रसादान्मयाच्युत।स्थितोऽस्मि गतसन्देहः करिष्ये वचनं तव ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Arjuna said: Destroyed is my delusion. I have gained knowledge through Thy grace, O Immutable one. I am firm, my doubts have fled away. I will do according to Thy word.
transliteration: arjuna uvācha naṣhṭo mohaḥ smṛitir labdhā tvat-prasādān mayāchyuta sthito ‘smi gata-sandehaḥ kariṣhye vachanaṁ tava

sanskrit: सञ्जय उवाचइत्यहं वासुदेवस्य पार्थस्य च महात्मनः।संवादमिममश्रौषमद्भुतं रोमहर्षणम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Sanjaya said: I heard this marvellous dialogue of Vâsudeva, and of the great-souled Partha, causing my hair to stand on end.
transliteration: sañjaya uvācha ity ahaṁ vāsudevasya pārthasya cha mahātmanaḥ saṁvādam imam aśhrauṣham adbhutaṁ roma-harṣhaṇam

sanskrit: व्यासप्रसादाच्छ्रुतवानेतद्गुह्यमहं परम्।योगं योगेश्वरात्कृष्णात्साक्षात्कथयतः स्वयम् ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: By the favour of Vyâsa I listened to this secret and supreme yoga from the Lord of Yoga, Krishna Himself, speaking before mine eyes.
transliteration: vyāsa-prasādāch chhrutavān etad guhyam ahaṁ param yogaṁ yogeśhvarāt kṛiṣhṇāt sākṣhāt kathayataḥ svayam

sanskrit: राजन्संस्मृत्य संस्मृत्य संवादमिममद्भुतम्।केशवार्जुनयोः पुण्यं हृष्यामि च मुहुर्मुहुः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: O King, remembering, remembering this marvellous and holy dialogue between Keshava and Arjuna, I rejoice again and again.
transliteration: rājan sansmṛitya sansmṛitya saṁvādam imam adbhutam keśhavārjunayoḥ puṇyaṁ hṛiṣhyāmi cha muhur muhuḥ

sanskrit: तच्च संस्मृत्य संस्मृत्य रूपमत्यद्भुतं हरेः। विस्मयो मे महान् राजन् हृष्यामि च पुनः पुनः ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Remembering, remembering, also that most marvellous form of Hari, great is my wonder, O King. I rejoice, again and again.
transliteration: tach cha sansmṛitya saṁsmṛitya rūpam aty-adbhutaṁ hareḥ vismayo ye mahān rājan hṛiṣhyāmi cha punaḥ punaḥ

sanskrit: यत्र योगेश्वरः कृष्णो यत्र पार्थो धनुर्धरः। तत्र श्रीर्विजयो भूतिर्ध्रुवा नीतिर्मतिर्मम ॥
sanskrit_source: gita/gita open dataset (github.com/gita/gita, data/verse.json); Devanagari + IAST; verse text is ancient/public-domain
text_en: Wherever is Krishna, Yoga's Lord, wherever is Pârtha, the archer, assured are there prosperity, victory and happiness. So I think.
transliteration: yatra yogeśhvaraḥ kṛiṣhṇo yatra pārtho dhanur-dharaḥ tatra śhrīr vijayo bhūtir dhruvā nītir matir mama
